# @dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2

[![npm](https://img.shields.io/badge/npm-v5.2.3-blue)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2/v/5.2.3)
[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache_2.0-blue.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0)


Documentation of the Dynatrace Environment API v2. Resources here generally supersede those in v1. Migration of resources from v1 is in progress. 

If you miss a resource, consider using the Dynatrace Environment API v1.

To read about use cases and examples, see [Dynatrace Documentation](https://dt-url.net/2u23k1k).

Notes about compatibility:
* Operations marked as early adopter or preview may be changed in non-compatible ways, although we try to avoid this.
* We may add new enum constants without incrementing the API version; thus, clients need to handle unknown enum constants gracefully.


## Installation

```bash
npm install @dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2
```


## Getting help

- Visit [SDK for Typescript](https://developer.dynatrace.com/reference/sdks/) guide in the [Dynatrace Developer](https://developer.dynatrace.com/)
- Ask a question in the [Dynatrace Community](https://community.dynatrace.com/)


## License

This SDK is distributed under the [Apache License, Version 2.0](http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0), see LICENSE for more information.


# API reference

Full API reference for the latest version of the SDK is also available at the [Dynatrace Developer](https://developer.dynatrace.com/reference/sdks/client-classic-environment-v2/).

## accessTokensActiveGateTokensClient

```js
import { accessTokensActiveGateTokensClient } from '@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2';
```


<a name="createtoken"></a>
### createToken

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>accessTokensActiveGateTokensClient.createToken(config): Promise&lt;<a href="#activegatetokencreated">ActiveGateTokenCreated</a>&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Creates a new ActiveGate token

**One of the following scopes is required:**
+ environment-api:activegate-tokens:create
+ environment-api:activegate-tokens:write


**Required permission:** environment:roles:manage-settings

The newly created token will be owned by the same user who owns the token used for authentication of the call.



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type |
| --- | --- |
|config.body<sup>*required</sup>|<a href="#activegatetokencreate" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">ActiveGateTokenCreate</a>| 




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[ActiveGateTokenCreated](#activegatetokencreated)|201|Success. The token has been created. The body of the response contains the token secret.|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Failed. The input is invalid. &#124; Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { accessTokensActiveGateTokensClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data =
  await accessTokensActiveGateTokensClient.createToken({
    body: {
      activeGateType: "ENVIRONMENT",
      name: "myToken",
    },
  });
```

</details>



</div>


<a name="gettoken"></a>
### getToken

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>accessTokensActiveGateTokensClient.getToken(config): Promise&lt;<a href="#activegatetoken">ActiveGateToken</a>&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Gets metadata of an ActiveGate token

**Required scope:** environment-api:activegate-tokens:read
**Required permission:** environment:roles:manage-settings

The token secret is **not** exposed.



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.activeGateTokenIdentifier<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The ActiveGate token identifier, consisting of <a href="https://dt-url.net/rn00tjg" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" >prefix and public part</a> of the token. |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[ActiveGateToken](#activegatetoken)|200|Success. The response contains the metadata of the tokens.|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Failed. The input is invalid. &#124; Failed. The requested resource doesn't exist. &#124; Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { accessTokensActiveGateTokensClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data =
  await accessTokensActiveGateTokensClient.getToken({
    activeGateTokenIdentifier: "...",
  });
```

</details>



</div>


<a name="listtokens"></a>
### listTokens

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>accessTokensActiveGateTokensClient.listTokens(config): Promise&lt;<a href="#activegatetokenlist" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">ActiveGateTokenList</a>&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Lists all available ActiveGate tokens

**Required scope:** environment-api:activegate-tokens:read
**Required permission:** environment:roles:manage-settings

You can limit the output by using pagination:
1. Specify the number of results per page in the **pageSize** query parameter.
2. Use the cursor from the **nextPageKey** field of the previous response in the **nextPageKey** query parameter to obtain subsequent pages.



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.nextPageKey|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>The cursor for the next page of results. You can find it in the <strong>nextPageKey</strong> field of the previous response.</p> <p>The first page is always returned if you don't specify the <strong>nextPageKey</strong> query parameter.</p> <p>When the <strong>nextPageKey</strong> is set to obtain subsequent pages, you must omit all other query parameters.</p>  |
|config.pageSize|[number](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Number)|<p>The amount of ActiveGate tokens in a single response payload.</p> <p>The maximal allowed page size is 3000 and the minimal size is 100.</p> <p>If not set, 100 is used.</p>  |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[ActiveGateTokenList](#activegatetokenlist)|200|Success. The response contains the list of ActiveGate tokens.|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Failed. The input is invalid. &#124; Failed. The requested resource doesn't exist. &#124; Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { accessTokensActiveGateTokensClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data =
  await accessTokensActiveGateTokensClient.listTokens();
```

</details>



</div>


<a name="revoketoken"></a>
### revokeToken

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>accessTokensActiveGateTokensClient.revokeToken(config): Promise&lt;void&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Deletes an ActiveGate token

**Required scope:** environment-api:activegate-tokens:write
**Required permission:** environment:roles:manage-settings



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.activeGateTokenIdentifier<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The ActiveGate token identifier, consisting of <a href="https://dt-url.net/rn00tjg" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" >prefix and public part</a> of the token to be deleted. |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|void|204|Success. Response doesn&apos;t have a body.|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Failed. The input is invalid. &#124; Failed. The requested resource doesn't exist. &#124; Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { accessTokensActiveGateTokensClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data =
  await accessTokensActiveGateTokensClient.revokeToken({
    activeGateTokenIdentifier: "...",
  });
```

</details>



</div>

## accessTokensApiTokensClient

```js
import { accessTokensApiTokensClient } from '@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2';
```


<a name="createapitoken"></a>
### createApiToken

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>accessTokensApiTokensClient.createApiToken(config): Promise&lt;<a href="#apitokencreated" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">ApiTokenCreated</a>&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Creates a new API token

**Required scope:** environment-api:api-tokens:write
**Required permission:** environment:roles:viewer

The newly created token will be owned by the same user who owns the token used for authentication of the call.

 Creating personal access tokens requires the `environment:roles:viewer` permission. Creating access tokens requires the `environment:roles:manage-settings` permission.



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type |
| --- | --- |
|config.body<sup>*required</sup>|<a href="#apitokencreate">ApiTokenCreate</a>| 




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[ApiTokenCreated](#apitokencreated)|201|Success. The token has been created. The body of the response contains the token secret.|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Failed. The input is invalid. &#124; Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { accessTokensApiTokensClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data =
  await accessTokensApiTokensClient.createApiToken({
    body: { name: "tokenName", scopes: ["metrics.read"] },
  });
```

</details>



</div>


<a name="deleteapitoken"></a>
### deleteApiToken

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>accessTokensApiTokensClient.deleteApiToken(config): Promise&lt;void&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Deletes an API token

**Required scope:** environment-api:api-tokens:write
**Required permission:** environment:roles:viewer



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.id<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>The ID of the token to be deleted.</p> <p>You can specify either the ID or the secret of the token.</p> <p>You can't delete the token you're using for authentication of the request.</p>  |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|void|204|Success. Response doesn&apos;t have a body.|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Failed. You can't delete the token you're using for authentication of the request. &#124; Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { accessTokensApiTokensClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data =
  await accessTokensApiTokensClient.deleteApiToken({
    id: "...",
  });
```

</details>



</div>


<a name="getapitoken"></a>
### getApiToken

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>accessTokensApiTokensClient.getApiToken(config): Promise&lt;<a href="#apitoken" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">ApiToken</a>&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Gets API token metadata by token ID

**Required scope:** environment-api:api-tokens:read
**Required permission:** environment:roles:viewer

The token secret is **not** exposed.



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type |
| --- | --- |
|config.id<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)| 




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[ApiToken](#apitoken)|200|Success|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Failed. The input is invalid. &#124; Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { accessTokensApiTokensClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data = await accessTokensApiTokensClient.getApiToken({
  id: "...",
});
```

</details>



</div>


<a name="listapitokens"></a>
### listApiTokens

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>accessTokensApiTokensClient.listApiTokens(config): Promise&lt;<a href="#apitokenlist">ApiTokenList</a>&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Lists all available API tokens

**Required scope:** environment-api:api-tokens:read
**Required permission:** environment:roles:viewer

You can limit the output by using pagination:
1. Specify the number of results per page in the **pageSize** query parameter.
2. Use the cursor from the **nextPageKey** field of the previous response in the **nextPageKey** query parameter to obtain subsequent pages.



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.apiTokenSelector|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>Filters the resulting sets of tokens. Only tokens matching the specified criteria are included into response.</p> <p>You can set one or more of the following criteria:</p> <ul> <li>Owner: <code>owner(&quot;value&quot;)</code>. The user that owns the token. Case-sensitive.</li> <li>Personal access token: <code>personalAccessToken(false)</code>. Set to <code>true</code> to include only personal access tokens or to <code>false</code> to include only API tokens.</li> <li>Token scope: <code>scope(&quot;scope1&quot;,&quot;scope2&quot;)</code>. If several values are specified, the <strong>OR</strong> logic applies.</li> </ul> <p>To set multiple criteria, separate them with commas (<code>,</code>). Only results matching <strong>all</strong> criteria are included into response.</p>  |
|config.fields|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>Specifies the fields to be included in the response.</p> <p>The following fields are included by default:</p> <ul> <li><code>id</code></li> <li><code>name</code></li> <li><code>enabled</code></li> <li><code>owner</code></li> <li><code>creationDate</code></li> </ul> <p>To remove fields from the response, specify them with the minus (<code>-</code>) operator as a comma-separated list (for example, <code>-creationDate,-owner</code>).</p> <p>You can include additional fields:</p> <ul> <li> <p><code>personalAccessToken</code></p> </li> <li> <p><code>expirationDate</code></p> </li> <li> <p><code>lastUsedDate</code></p> </li> <li> <p><code>lastUsedIpAddress</code></p> </li> <li> <p><code>modifiedDate</code></p> </li> <li> <p><code>scopes</code></p> </li> <li> <p><code>additionalMetadata</code></p> </li> </ul> <p>To add fields to the response, specify them with the plus (<code>+</code>) operator as a comma-separated list (for example, <code>+expirationDate,+scopes</code>). You can combine adding and removing of fields (for example, <code>+scopes,-creationDate</code>).</p> <p>Alternatively, you can define the desired set of fields in the response. Specify the required fields as a comma-separated list, without operators (for example, <code>creationDate,expirationDate,owner</code>). The ID is always included in the response.</p> <p>The <strong>fields</strong> string must be URL-encoded.</p>  |
|config.from|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>Filters tokens based on the last usage time. The start of the requested timeframe.</p> <p>You can use one of the following formats:</p> <ul> <li>Timestamp in UTC milliseconds.</li> <li>Human-readable format of <code>2021-01-25T05:57:01.123+01:00</code>. If no time zone is specified, UTC is used. You can use a space character instead of the <code>T</code>. Seconds and fractions of a second are optional.</li> <li>Relative timeframe, back from now. The format is <code>now-NU/A</code>, where <code>N</code> is the amount of time, <code>U</code> is the unit of time, and <code>A</code> is an alignment. The alignment rounds all the smaller values to the nearest zero in the past. For example, <code>now-1y/w</code> is one year back, aligned by a week. You can also specify relative timeframe without an alignment: <code>now-NU</code>. Supported time units for the relative timeframe are: <ul> <li><code>m</code>: minutes</li> <li><code>h</code>: hours</li> <li><code>d</code>: days</li> <li><code>w</code>: weeks</li> <li><code>M</code>: months</li> <li><code>y</code>: years</li> </ul> </li> </ul>  |
|config.nextPageKey|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>The cursor for the next page of results. You can find it in the <strong>nextPageKey</strong> field of the previous response.</p> <p>The first page is always returned if you don't specify the <strong>nextPageKey</strong> query parameter.</p> <p>When the <strong>nextPageKey</strong> is set to obtain subsequent pages, you must omit all other query parameters.</p>  |
|config.pageSize|[number](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Number)|<p>The amount of API tokens in a single response payload.</p> <p>The maximal allowed page size is 10000 and the minimal allowed page size is 100.</p> <p>If not set, 200 is used.</p>  |
|config.sort|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>The sort order of the token list.</p> <p>You can sort by the following properties with a sign prefix for the sort order:</p> <ul> <li><code>name</code>: token name (<code>+</code> a...z or <code>-</code> z...a)</li> <li><code>lastUsedDate</code> last used (<code>+</code> never used tokens first <code>-</code> most recently used tokens first)</li> <li><code>creationDate</code> (<code>+</code> oldest tokens first <code>-</code> newest tokens first)</li> <li><code>expirationDate</code> (<code>+</code> tokens that expire soon first <code>-</code> unlimited tokens first)</li> <li><code>modifiedDate</code> last modified (<code>+</code> never modified tokens first <code>-</code> most recently modified tokens first)</li> </ul> <p>If no prefix is set, + is used.</p> <p>If not set, tokens are sorted by creation date with newest first.</p>  |
|config.to|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>Filters tokens based on the last usage time. The end of the requested timeframe.</p> <p>You can use one of the following formats:</p> <ul> <li>Timestamp in UTC milliseconds.</li> <li>Human-readable format of <code>2021-01-25T05:57:01.123+01:00</code>. If no time zone is specified, UTC is used. You can use a space character instead of the <code>T</code>. Seconds and fractions of a second are optional.</li> <li>Relative timeframe, back from now. The format is <code>now-NU/A</code>, where <code>N</code> is the amount of time, <code>U</code> is the unit of time, and <code>A</code> is an alignment. The alignment rounds all the smaller values to the nearest zero in the past. For example, <code>now-1y/w</code> is one year back, aligned by a week. You can also specify relative timeframe without an alignment: <code>now-NU</code>. Supported time units for the relative timeframe are: <ul> <li><code>m</code>: minutes</li> <li><code>h</code>: hours</li> <li><code>d</code>: days</li> <li><code>w</code>: weeks</li> <li><code>M</code>: months</li> <li><code>y</code>: years</li> </ul> </li> </ul> <p>If not set, the current timestamp is used.</p>  |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[ApiTokenList](#apitokenlist)|200|Success|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Failed. The input is invalid. &#124; Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { accessTokensApiTokensClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data =
  await accessTokensApiTokensClient.listApiTokens();
```

</details>



</div>


<a name="lookupapitoken"></a>
### lookupApiToken

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>accessTokensApiTokensClient.lookupApiToken(config): Promise&lt;<a href="#apitoken" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">ApiToken</a>&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Gets API token metadata by token secret

**Required scope:** environment-api:api-tokens:read
**Required permission:** environment:roles:viewer



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type |
| --- | --- |
|config.body<sup>*required</sup>|<a href="#apitokensecret">ApiTokenSecret</a>| 




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[ApiToken](#apitoken)|200|Success|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { accessTokensApiTokensClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data =
  await accessTokensApiTokensClient.lookupApiToken({
    body: {
      token:
        "dt0c01.ST2EY72KQINMH574WMNVI7YN.G3DFPBEJYMODIDAEX454M7YWBUVEFOWKPRVMWFASS64NFH52PX6BNDVFFM572RZM",
    },
  });
```

</details>



</div>


<a name="updateapitoken"></a>
### updateApiToken

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>accessTokensApiTokensClient.updateApiToken(config): Promise&lt;void&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Updates an API token

**Required scope:** environment-api:api-tokens:write
**Required permission:** environment:roles:viewer



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.body<sup>*required</sup>|<a href="#apitokenupdate" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">ApiTokenUpdate</a>| |
|config.id<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>The ID of the token to be updated.</p> <p>You can't disable the token you're using for authentication of the request.</p>  |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|void|204|Success. Response doesn&apos;t have a body.|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Failed. The input is invalid. &#124; Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { accessTokensApiTokensClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data =
  await accessTokensApiTokensClient.updateApiToken({
    id: "...",
    body: {},
  });
```

</details>



</div>

## accessTokensTenantTokensClient

```js
import { accessTokensTenantTokensClient } from '@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2';
```


<a name="cancelrotation"></a>
### cancelRotation

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>accessTokensTenantTokensClient.cancelRotation(config): Promise&lt;<a href="#tenanttokenconfig">TenantTokenConfig</a>&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Cancels tenant token rotation

**Required scope:** environment-api:tenant-token-rotation:write
**Required permission:** environment:roles:manage-settings

To learn how to rotate tokens, see [Token rotation](https://dt-url.net/1543sf6) in Dynatrace Documentation.



</div>



#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[TenantTokenConfig](#tenanttokenconfig)|200|Success. Rotation process has been cancelled. The current tenant token remains valid.|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Failed. There is no ongoing rotation process. &#124; Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { accessTokensTenantTokensClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data =
  await accessTokensTenantTokensClient.cancelRotation();
```

</details>



</div>


<a name="finishrotation"></a>
### finishRotation

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>accessTokensTenantTokensClient.finishRotation(config): Promise&lt;<a href="#tenanttokenconfig" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">TenantTokenConfig</a>&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Finishes tenant token rotation

**Required scope:** environment-api:tenant-token-rotation:write
**Required permission:** environment:roles:manage-settings

To learn how to rotate tokens, see [Token rotation](https://dt-url.net/1543sf6) in Dynatrace Documentation.



</div>



#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[TenantTokenConfig](#tenanttokenconfig)|200|Success. The rotation process is completed. The **active** field of the response contains the new tenant token.|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|No ongoing rotation process. &#124; Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { accessTokensTenantTokensClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data =
  await accessTokensTenantTokensClient.finishRotation();
```

</details>



</div>


<a name="startrotation"></a>
### startRotation

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>accessTokensTenantTokensClient.startRotation(config): Promise&lt;<a href="#tenanttokenconfig">TenantTokenConfig</a>&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Starts tenant token rotation

**Required scope:** environment-api:tenant-token-rotation:write
**Required permission:** environment:roles:manage-settings

To learn how to rotate tokens, see [Token rotation](https://dt-url.net/1543sf6) in Dynatrace Documentation.



</div>



#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[TenantTokenConfig](#tenanttokenconfig)|200|Success. The new tenant token is created and will replace the old one. The **active** field of the response contains the new tenant token.|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Failed. Another rotation process is already in progress. &#124; Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { accessTokensTenantTokensClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data =
  await accessTokensTenantTokensClient.startRotation();
```

</details>



</div>

<a name="getallupdatejoblist"></a>
### getAllUpdateJobList

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>activeGatesAutoUpdateJobsClient.getAllUpdateJobList(config): Promise&lt;<a href="#updatejobsall">UpdateJobsAll</a>&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

List ActiveGates with update jobs

**Required scope:** environment-api:activegates:read
**Required permission:** environment:roles:manage-settings

The response includes ActiveGates that have both completed (successful and failed) jobs and jobs in progress.



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.from|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>The start of the requested timeframe for update jobs.</p> <p>You can use one of the following formats:</p> <ul> <li>Timestamp in UTC milliseconds.</li> <li>Human-readable format of <code>2021-01-25T05:57:01.123+01:00</code>. If no time zone is specified, UTC is used. You can use a space character instead of the <code>T</code>. Seconds and fractions of a second are optional.</li> <li>Relative timeframe, back from now. The format is <code>now-NU/A</code>, where <code>N</code> is the amount of time, <code>U</code> is the unit of time, and <code>A</code> is an alignment. The alignment rounds all the smaller values to the nearest zero in the past. For example, <code>now-1y/w</code> is one year back, aligned by a week. You can also specify relative timeframe without an alignment: <code>now-NU</code>. Supported time units for the relative timeframe are: <ul> <li><code>m</code>: minutes</li> <li><code>h</code>: hours</li> <li><code>d</code>: days</li> <li><code>w</code>: weeks</li> <li><code>M</code>: months</li> <li><code>y</code>: years</li> </ul> </li> </ul> <p>If not set, the relative timeframe of one day is used (<code>now-1d</code>).</p> <p>Maximum timeframe is 31 days.</p>  |
|config.lastUpdates|[boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Boolean)|If <code>true</code>, filters the resulting set of update jobs to the most recent update of each type. |
|config.startVersion|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Filters the resulting set of update-jobs by the initial version (required format <code>&lt;major&gt;.&lt;minor&gt;.&lt;revision&gt;</code>). |
|config.startVersionCompareType|"EQUAL" &#124; "GREATER" &#124; "GREATER_EQUAL" &#124; "LOWER" &#124; "LOWER_EQUAL"|<p>Filters the resulting set of update jobs by the specified initial version.</p> <p>Specify the comparison operator here.</p>  |
|config.targetVersion|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Filters the resulting set of update-jobs by the target version (required format <code>&lt;major&gt;.&lt;minor&gt;.&lt;revision&gt;</code>). |
|config.targetVersionCompareType|"EQUAL" &#124; "GREATER" &#124; "GREATER_EQUAL" &#124; "LOWER" &#124; "LOWER_EQUAL"|<p>Filters the resulting set of update jobs by the specified target version.</p> <p>Specify the comparison operator here.</p>  |
|config.to|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>The end of the requested timeframe for update jobs.</p> <p>You can use one of the following formats:</p> <ul> <li>Timestamp in UTC milliseconds.</li> <li>Human-readable format of <code>2021-01-25T05:57:01.123+01:00</code>. If no time zone is specified, UTC is used. You can use a space character instead of the <code>T</code>. Seconds and fractions of a second are optional.</li> <li>Relative timeframe, back from now. The format is <code>now-NU/A</code>, where <code>N</code> is the amount of time, <code>U</code> is the unit of time, and <code>A</code> is an alignment. The alignment rounds all the smaller values to the nearest zero in the past. For example, <code>now-1y/w</code> is one year back, aligned by a week. You can also specify relative timeframe without an alignment: <code>now-NU</code>. Supported time units for the relative timeframe are: <ul> <li><code>m</code>: minutes</li> <li><code>h</code>: hours</li> <li><code>d</code>: days</li> <li><code>w</code>: weeks</li> <li><code>M</code>: months</li> <li><code>y</code>: years</li> </ul> </li> </ul> <p>If not set, the current timestamp is used.</p>  |
|config.updateType|"SYNTHETIC" &#124; "ACTIVE_GATE" &#124; "REMOTE_PLUGIN_AGENT" &#124; "Z_REMOTE"|Filters the resulting set of update-jobs by the update type. |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[UpdateJobsAll](#updatejobsall)|200|Success|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Failed. The input is invalid. &#124; Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { activeGatesAutoUpdateJobsClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data =
  await activeGatesAutoUpdateJobsClient.getAllUpdateJobList();
```

</details>



</div>

The format is <code>now-NU/A</code>, where <code>N</code> is the amount of time, <code>U</code> is the unit of time, and <code>A</code> is an alignment. The alignment rounds all the smaller values to the nearest zero in the past. For example, <code>now-1y/w</code> is one year back, aligned by a week. You can also specify relative timeframe without an alignment: <code>now-NU</code>. Supported time units for the relative timeframe are: <ul> <li><code>m</code>: minutes</li> <li><code>h</code>: hours</li> <li><code>d</code>: days</li> <li><code>w</code>: weeks</li> <li><code>M</code>: months</li> <li><code>y</code>: years</li> </ul> </li> </ul> <p>If not set, the relative timeframe of one week is used (<code>now-1w</code>).</p> <p>Maximum timeframe is 31 days.</p>  |
|config.lastUpdates|[boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Boolean)|If <code>true</code>, filters the resulting set of update jobs to the most recent update of each type. |
|config.startVersion|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Filters the resulting set of update-jobs by the initial version (required format <code>&lt;major&gt;.&lt;minor&gt;.&lt;revision&gt;</code>). |

<a name="validateupdatejobforag"></a>
### validateUpdateJobForAg

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>activeGatesAutoUpdateJobsClient.validateUpdateJobForAg(config): Promise&lt;void&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Validates the payload for the `POST /activeGates/{agId}/updateJobs` request.

**Required scope:** environment-api:activegates:write
**Required permission:** environment:roles:manage-settings



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.agId<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The ID of the required ActiveGate. |
|config.body<sup>*required</sup>|<a href="#updatejob" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">UpdateJob</a>| |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|void|204|Validated. The submitted update-job is valid. Response doesn&apos;t have a body.|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Failed. The input is invalid. &#124; Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { activeGatesAutoUpdateJobsClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data =
  await activeGatesAutoUpdateJobsClient.validateUpdateJobForAg(
    {
      agId: "...",
      body: { targetVersion: "1.190.0.20200301-130000" },
    },
  );
```

</details>



</div>

## activeGatesClient

```js
import { activeGatesClient } from '@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2';
```


<a name="getallactivegates"></a>
### getAllActiveGates

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>activeGatesClient.getAllActiveGates(config): Promise&lt;<a href="#activegatelist">ActiveGateList</a>&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Lists all available ActiveGates

**Required scope:** environment-api:activegates:read
**Required permission:** environment:roles:manage-settings

The response includes all ActiveGates that are currently connected to the environment or have been connected during last 2 hours.



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.autoUpdate|"ENABLED" &#124; "DISABLED"|Filters the resulting set of ActiveGates by the actual state of auto-update. |
|config.containerized|[boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Boolean)|Filters the resulting set of ActiveGates to those which are running in container (<code>true</code>) or not (<code>false</code>). |
|config.disabledModule|Array&lt;"SYNTHETIC" &#124; "AWS" &#124; "AZURE" &#124; "BEACON_FORWARDER" &#124; "CLOUD_FOUNDRY" &#124; "DB_INSIGHT" &#124; "DEBUGGING" &#124; "EXTENSIONS_V1" &#124; "EXTENSIONS_V2" &#124; "KUBERNETES" &#124; "LOGS" &#124; "MEMORY_DUMPS" &#124; "METRIC_API" &#124; "ONE_AGENT_ROUTING" &#124; "OTLP_INGEST" &#124; "REST_API" &#124; "VMWARE" &#124; "Z_OS"&gt;|Filters the resulting set of ActiveGates by the disabled modules. |
|config.enabledModule|Array&lt;"SYNTHETIC" &#124; "AWS" &#124; "AZURE" &#124; "BEACON_FORWARDER" &#124; "CLOUD_FOUNDRY" &#124; "DB_INSIGHT" &#124; "DEBUGGING" &#124; "EXTENSIONS_V1" &#124; "EXTENSIONS_V2" &#124; "KUBERNETES" &#124; "LOGS" &#124; "MEMORY_DUMPS" &#124; "METRIC_API" &#124; "ONE_AGENT_ROUTING" &#124; "OTLP_INGEST" &#124; "REST_API" &#124; "VMWARE" &#124; "Z_OS"&gt;|Filters the resulting set of ActiveGates by the enabled modules. |
|config.fipsMode|[boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Boolean)|Filters the resulting set of ActiveGates to those which are running in FIPS mode (<code>true</code>) or not (<code>false</code>). |
|config.group|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>Filters the resulting set of ActiveGates by the group.</p> <p>You can specify a partial name. In that case, the <code>CONTAINS</code> operator is used.</p>  |
|config.hostname|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>Filters the resulting set of ActiveGates by the name of the host it's running on.</p> <p>You can specify a partial name. In that case, the <code>CONTAINS</code> operator is used.</p>  |
|config.loadBalancerAddress|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>Filters the resulting set of ActiveGates by the Load Balancer address.</p> <p>You can specify a partial address. In that case, the <code>CONTAINS</code> operator is used.</p>  |
|config.networkAddress|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>Filters the resulting set of ActiveGates by the network address.</p> <p>You can specify a partial address. In that case, the <code>CONTAINS</code> operator is used.</p>  |
|config.networkZone|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>Filters the resulting set of ActiveGates by the network zone.</p> <p>You can specify a partial name. In that case, the <code>CONTAINS</code> operator is used.</p>  |
|config.online|[boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Boolean)|Filters the resulting set of ActiveGates by the communication status. |
|config.osArchitecture|"S390" &#124; "X86" &#124; "ARM" &#124; "PPCLE"|Filters the resulting set of ActiveGates by the OS architecture of the host it's running on. |
|config.osType|"LINUX" &#124; "WINDOWS"|Filters the resulting set of ActiveGates by the OS type of the host it's running on. |
|config.tokenExpirationSet|[boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Boolean)|Filters the resulting set of ActiveGates to those with set expiration date for authorization token. |
|config.tokenState|"UNKNOWN" &#124; "ABSENT" &#124; "EXPIRING" &#124; "INVALID" &#124; "UNSUPPORTED" &#124; "VALID"|Filters the resulting set of ActiveGates to those with authorization token in specified state. |
|config.type|"ENVIRONMENT" &#124; "ENVIRONMENT_MULTI"|Filters the resulting set of ActiveGates by the ActiveGate type. |
|config.updateStatus|"UNKNOWN" &#124; "INCOMPATIBLE" &#124; "OUTDATED" &#124; "SCHEDULED" &#124; "SUPPRESSED" &#124; "UP2DATE" &#124; "UPDATE_IN_PROGRESS" &#124; "UPDATE_PENDING" &#124; "UPDATE_PROBLEM"|Filters the resulting set of ActiveGates by the auto-update status. |
|config.version|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>Filters the resulting set of ActiveGates by the specified version.</p> <p>Specify the version in <code>&lt;major&gt;.&lt;minor&gt;.&lt;revision&gt;</code> format (for example, <code>1.195.0</code>) here.</p>  |
|config.versionCompareType|"EQUAL" &#124; "GREATER" &#124; "GREATER_EQUAL" &#124; "LOWER" &#124; "LOWER_EQUAL"|<p>Filters the resulting set of ActiveGates by the specified version.</p> <p>Specify the comparison operator here.</p>  |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[ActiveGateList](#activegatelist)|200|Success|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Failed. The input is invalid. &#124; Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { activeGatesClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data = await activeGatesClient.getAllActiveGates();
```

</details>



</div>


<a name="getoneactivegatebyid"></a>
### getOneActiveGateById

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>activeGatesClient.getOneActiveGateById(config): Promise&lt;<a href="#activegate" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">ActiveGate</a>&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Gets the details of the specified ActiveGate

**Required scope:** environment-api:activegates:read
**Required permission:** environment:roles:manage-settings



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.agId<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The ID of the required ActiveGate. |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[ActiveGate](#activegate)|200|Success|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Not found. See response body for details. &#124; Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { activeGatesClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

  agId: "...",
});
```

</details>

## attacksClient

```js
import { attacksClient } from '@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2';
```


<a name="getattack"></a>
### getAttack

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>attacksClient.getAttack(config): Promise&lt;<a href="#attack">Attack</a>&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Gets the specified attack

**Required scope:** environment-api:attacks:read
**One of the following permissions is required:**
+ environment:roles:manage-security-problems
+ environment:roles:view-security-problems



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.fields|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>A list of additional attack properties you can add to the response.</p> <p>The following properties are available (all other properties are always included and you can't remove them from the response):</p> <ul> <li><code>attackTarget</code>: The targeted host/database of an attack.</li> <li><code>request</code>: The request that was sent from the attacker.</li> <li><code>entrypoint</code>: The entry point used by an attacker to start a specific attack.</li> <li><code>vulnerability</code>: The vulnerability utilized by the attack.</li> <li><code>securityProblem</code>: The related security problem.</li> <li><code>attacker</code>: The attacker of an attack.</li> <li><code>managementZones</code>: The related management zones.</li> </ul> <p>To add properties, specify them in a comma-separated list and prefix each property with a plus (for example, <code>+attackTarget,+securityProblem</code>).</p>  |
|config.id<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The ID of the attack. |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[Attack](#attack)|200|Success|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { attacksClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data = await attacksClient.getAttack({ id: "..." });
```

</details>



</div>


<a name="getattacks"></a>
### getAttacks

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>attacksClient.getAttacks(config): Promise&lt;<a href="#attacklist" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">AttackList</a>&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Lists all attacks

**Required scope:** environment-api:attacks:read
**One of the following permissions is required:**
+ environment:roles:manage-security-problems
+ environment:roles:view-security-problems



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.attackSelector|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>Defines the scope of the query. Only attacks matching the specified criteria are included in the response. You can add one or more of the following criteria. Values are <em>not</em> case-sensitive and the <code>EQUALS</code> operator is used unless otherwise specified.</p> <ul> <li>State: <code>state(&quot;value&quot;)</code>. The state of the attack. Possible values are <code>EXPLOITED</code>, <code>BLOCKED</code>, and <code>ALLOWLISTED</code>.</li> <li>Attack Type: <code>attackType(&quot;value&quot;)</code>. The type of the attack. Find the possible values in the description of the <strong>attackType</strong> field of the response.</li> <li>Country Code: <code>countryCode(&quot;value&quot;)</code>. The country code of the attacker. Supported values include all ISO-3166-1 alpha-2 country codes (2-letter). Supplying empty filter value <code>countryCode()</code> will return attacks, where location is not available.</li> <li>Request path contains: <code>requestPathContains(&quot;value&quot;)</code>. Filters for a substring in the request path. The <code>CONTAINS</code> operator is used. A maximum of 48 characters are allowed.</li> <li>Process group name contains: <code>processGroupNameContains(&quot;value&quot;)</code>. Filters for a substring in the targeted process group's name. The <code>CONTAINS</code> operator is used.</li> <li>Vulnerability ID: <code>vulnerabilityId(&quot;123456789&quot;)</code>. The exact ID of the vulnerability.</li> <li>Source IPs: <code>sourceIps(&quot;93.184.216.34&quot;, &quot;63.124.6.12&quot;)</code>. The exact IPv4/IPv6 addresses of the attacker.</li> <li>Management zone ID: <code>managementZoneIds(&quot;mzId-1&quot;, &quot;mzId-2&quot;)</code>.</li> <li>Management zone name: <code>managementZones(&quot;name-1&quot;, &quot;name-2&quot;)</code>. Values are case sensitive.</li> <li>Technology: <code>technology(&quot;technology-1&quot;, &quot;technology-2&quot;)</code>. Find the possible values in the description of the <strong>technology</strong> field of the response. The <code>EQUALS</code> operator is used.</li> </ul> <p>To set several criteria, separate them with a comma (<code>,</code>). Only results matching (<strong>all</strong> criteria are included in the response.</p> <p>Specify the value of a criterion as a quoted string. The following special characters must be escaped with a tilde (<code>&#126;</code>) inside quotes:</p> <ul> <li>Tilde <code>&#126;</code></li> <li>Quote <code>&quot;</code></li> </ul>  |
|config.fields|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>A list of additional attack properties you can add to the response.</p> <p>The following properties are available (all other properties are always included and you can't remove them from the response):</p> <ul> <li><code>attackTarget</code>: The targeted host/database of an attack.</li> <li><code>request</code>: The request that was sent from the attacker.</li> <li><code>entrypoint</code>: The entry point used by an attacker to start a specific attack.</li> <li><code>vulnerability</code>: The vulnerability utilized by the attack.</li> <li><code>securityProblem</code>: The related security problem.</li> <li><code>attacker</code>: The attacker of an attack.</li> <li><code>managementZones</code>: The related management zones.</li> <li><code>affectedEntities</code>: The affected entities of an attack.</li> </ul> <p>To add properties, specify them in a comma-separated list and prefix each property with a plus (for example, <code>+attackTarget,+securityProblem</code>).</p>  |
|config.from|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>The start of the requested timeframe.</p> <p>You can use one of the following formats:</p> <ul> <li>Timestamp in UTC milliseconds.</li> <li>Human-readable format of <code>2021-01-25T05:57:01.123+01:00</code>. If no time zone is specified, UTC is used. You can use a space character instead of the <code>T</code>. Seconds and fractions of a second are optional.</li> <li>Relative timeframe, back from now. The format is <code>now-NU/A</code>, where <code>N</code> is the amount of time, <code>U</code> is the unit of time, and <code>A</code> is an alignment. The alignment rounds all the smaller values to the nearest zero in the past. For example, <code>now-1y/w</code> is one year back, aligned by a week. You can also specify relative timeframe without an alignment: <code>now-NU</code>. Supported time units for the relative timeframe are: <ul> <li><code>m</code>: minutes</li> <li><code>h</code>: hours</li> <li><code>d</code>: days</li> <li><code>w</code>: weeks</li> <li><code>M</code>: months</li> <li><code>y</code>: years</li> </ul> </li> </ul> <p>If not set, the relative timeframe of thirty days is used (<code>now-30d</code>).</p>  |
|config.nextPageKey|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>The cursor for the next page of results. You can find it in the <strong>nextPageKey</strong> field of the previous response.</p> <p>The first page is always returned if you don't specify the <strong>nextPageKey</strong> query parameter.</p> <p>When the <strong>nextPageKey</strong> is set to obtain subsequent pages, you must omit all other query parameters.</p>  |
|config.pageSize|[number](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Number)|<p>The amount of attacks in a single response payload.</p> <p>The maximal allowed page size is 500.</p> <p>If not set, 100 is used.</p>  |
|config.sort|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>Specifies one or more fields for sorting the attack list. Multiple fields can be concatenated using a comma (<code>,</code>) as a separator (e.g. <code>+state,-timestamp</code>).</p> <p>You can sort by the following properties with a sign prefix for the sorting order.</p> <ul> <li><code>displayId</code>: The attack's display ID.</li> <li><code>displayName</code>: The attack's display name.</li> <li><code>attackType</code>: The type of the attack (e.g. SQL_INJECTION, JNDI_INJECTION, etc.).</li> <li><code>state</code>: The state of the attack. (<code>+</code> low severity state first <code>-</code> high severity state first)</li> <li><code>sourceIp</code>: The IP address of the attacker. Sorts by the numerical IP value.</li> <li><code>requestPath</code>: The request path where the attack was started.</li> <li><code>timestamp</code>: When the attack was executed. (<code>+</code> old attacks first or <code>-</code> new attacks first) If no prefix is set, <code>+</code> is used.</li> </ul>  |
|config.to|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>The end of the requested timeframe.</p> <p>You can use one of the following formats:</p> <ul> <li>Timestamp in UTC milliseconds.</li> <li>Human-readable format of <code>2021-01-25T05:57:01.123+01:00</code>. If no time zone is specified, UTC is used. You can use a space character instead of the <code>T</code>. Seconds and fractions of a second are optional.</li> <li>Relative timeframe, back from now. The format is <code>now-NU/A</code>, where <code>N</code> is the amount of time, <code>U</code> is the unit of time, and <code>A</code> is an alignment. The alignment rounds all the smaller values to the nearest zero in the past. For example, <code>now-1y/w</code> is one year back, aligned by a week. You can also specify relative timeframe without an alignment: <code>now-NU</code>. Supported time units for the relative timeframe are: <ul> <li><code>m</code>: minutes</li> <li><code>h</code>: hours</li> <li><code>d</code>: days</li> <li><code>w</code>: weeks</li> <li><code>M</code>: months</li> <li><code>y</code>: years</li> </ul> </li> </ul> <p>If not set, the current timestamp is used.</p>  |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[AttackList](#attacklist)|200|Success|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { attacksClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data = await attacksClient.getAttacks();
```

</details>



</div>

## auditLogsClient

```js
import { auditLogsClient } from '@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2';
```

<a name="getlog"></a>
### getLog

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>auditLogsClient.getLog(config): Promise&lt;<a href="#auditlogentry">AuditLogEntry</a>&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Gets the specified entry of the audit log | maturity=EARLY_ADOPTER

**Required scope:** environment-api:audit-logs:read
**Required permission:** environment:roles:manage-settings



</div>

| Name | Type | Description |

Code example

```ts
import { auditLogsClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

```

</div>


<a name="getlogs"></a>
### getLogs

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>auditLogsClient.getLogs(config): Promise&lt;<a href="#auditlog" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">AuditLog</a>&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Gets the audit log of your Dynatrace environment | maturity=EARLY_ADOPTER

**Required scope:** environment-api:audit-logs:read
**Required permission:** environment:roles:manage-settings

You can limit the output by using pagination:
1. Specify the number of results per page in the **pageSize** query parameter.
2. Use the cursor from the **nextPageKey** field of the previous response in the **nextPageKey** query parameter to obtain subsequent pages.



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.filter|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>Filters the audit log. You can use the following criteria:</p> <ul> <li>User: <code>user(&quot;userIdentification&quot;)</code>. The <code>EQUALS</code> operator applies.</li> <li>Event type: <code>eventType(&quot;value&quot;)</code>. The <code>EQUALS</code> operator applies.</li> <li>Category of a logged operation: <code>category(&quot;value&quot;)</code>. The <code>EQUALS</code> operator applies.</li> <li>Entity ID: <code>entityId(&quot;id&quot;)</code>. The <code>CONTAINS</code> operator applies.</li> <li>Settings schema ID: <code>dt.settings.schema_id(&quot;id&quot;)</code>. The <code>EQUALS</code> operator applies.</li> <li>Settings scope ID: <code>dt.settings.scope_id(&quot;id&quot;)</code>. The <code>EQUALS</code> operator applies.</li> <li>Settings key: <code>dt.settings.key(&quot;key&quot;)</code>. The <code>EQUALS</code> operator applies.</li> <li>Settings object ID: <code>dt.settings.object_id(&quot;id&quot;)</code>. The <code>EQUALS</code> operator applies.</li> </ul> <p>For each criterion, you can specify multiple alternatives with comma-separated values. In this case, the OR logic applies. For example, <code>eventType(&quot;CREATE&quot;,&quot;UPDATE&quot;)</code> means eventType can be &quot;CREATE&quot; or &quot;UPDATE&quot;.</p> <p>You can specify multiple comma-separated criteria, such as <code>eventType(&quot;CREATE&quot;,&quot;UPDATE&quot;),category(&quot;CONFIG&quot;)</code>. Only results matching <strong>all</strong> criteria are included in response.</p> <p>Specify the value of a criterion as a quoted string. The following special characters must be escaped with a tilde (<code>&#126;</code>) inside quotes:</p> <ul> <li>Tilde <code>&#126;</code></li> <li>Quote <code>&quot;</code></li> </ul>  |
|config.from|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>The start of the requested timeframe.</p> <p>You can use one of the following formats:</p> <ul> <li>Timestamp in UTC milliseconds.</li> <li>Human-readable format of <code>2021-01-25T05:57:01.123+01:00</code>. If no time zone is specified, UTC is used. You can use a space character instead of the <code>T</code>. Seconds and fractions of a second are optional.</li> <li>Relative timeframe, back from now. The format is <code>now-NU/A</code>, where <code>N</code> is the amount of time, <code>U</code> is the unit of time, and <code>A</code> is an alignment. The alignment rounds all the smaller values to the nearest zero in the past. For example, <code>now-1y/w</code> is one year back, aligned by a week. You can also specify relative timeframe without an alignment: <code>now-NU</code>. Supported time units for the relative timeframe are: <ul> <li><code>m</code>: minutes</li> <li><code>h</code>: hours</li> <li><code>d</code>: days</li> <li><code>w</code>: weeks</li> <li><code>M</code>: months</li> <li><code>y</code>: years</li> </ul> </li> </ul> <p>If not set, the relative timeframe of two weeks is used (<code>now-2w</code>).</p>  |
|config.nextPageKey|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>The cursor for the next page of results. You can find it in the <strong>nextPageKey</strong> field of the previous response.</p> <p>The first page is always returned if you don't specify the <strong>nextPageKey</strong> query parameter.</p> <p>When the <strong>nextPageKey</strong> is set to obtain subsequent pages, you must omit all other query parameters.</p>  |
|config.pageSize|[number](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Number)|<p>The amount of log entries in a single response payload.</p> <p>The maximal allowed page size is 5000.</p> <p>If not set, 1000 is used.</p>  |
|config.sort|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>The sorting of audit log entries:</p> <ul> <li><code>timestamp</code>: Oldest first.</li> <li><code>-timestamp</code>: Newest first.</li> </ul> <p>If not set, the newest first sorting is applied.</p>  |
|config.to|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>The end of the requested timeframe.</p> <p>You can use one of the following formats:</p> <ul> <li>Timestamp in UTC milliseconds.</li> <li>Human-readable format of <code>2021-01-25T05:57:01.123+01:00</code>. If no time zone is specified, UTC is used. You can use a space character instead of the <code>T</code>. Seconds and fractions of a second are optional.</li> <li>Relative timeframe, back from now. The format is <code>now-NU/A</code>, where <code>N</code> is the amount of time, <code>U</code> is the unit of time, and <code>A</code> is an alignment. The alignment rounds all the smaller values to the nearest zero in the past. For example, <code>now-1y/w</code> is one year back, aligned by a week. You can also specify relative timeframe without an alignment: <code>now-NU</code>. Supported time units for the relative timeframe are: <ul> <li><code>m</code>: minutes</li> <li><code>h</code>: hours</li> <li><code>d</code>: days</li> <li><code>w</code>: weeks</li> <li><code>M</code>: months</li> <li><code>y</code>: years</li> </ul> </li> </ul> <p>If not set, the current timestamp is used.</p>  |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[AuditLog](#auditlog)|200|Success|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { auditLogsClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data = await auditLogsClient.getLogs();
```

</details>



</div>

## businessEventsClient

```js
import { businessEventsClient } from '@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2';
```


<a name="ingest"></a>
### ingest

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>businessEventsClient.ingest(config): Promise&lt;void&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Ingests a business event

**Required scope:** storage:events:write
**Required permission:** storage:events:write

The maximum payload size of a single request is **5 MiB**. Requests with a greater payload are rejected, and the API returns a 413 response code.

The ingestion of business events is subject to licensing (see [licensing documentation](https://www.dynatrace.com/support/help/shortlink/dps-events)).



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type |
| --- | --- |
|config.body<sup>*required</sup>|<a href="#cloudevent">CloudEvent</a> &#124; Array&lt;<a href="#cloudevent" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">CloudEvent</a>&gt; &#124; IngestBody| 
|config.type<sup>*required</sup>|"application/cloudevent+json" &#124; "application/cloudevents+json" &#124; "application/cloudevent-batch+json" &#124; "application/cloudevents-batch+json" &#124; "application/json; charset=utf-8"| 




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|void|202|The provided business events are all accepted and will be processed.|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|BizEventIngestResultError|Some business events are invalid. Valid business events are accepted and will be processed. &#124; Content too large &#124; Too many requests &#124; Service is temporarily unavailable|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { businessEventsClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data = await businessEventsClient.ingest({
  type: "application/cloudevent+json",
  body: {
    specversion: "1.0",
    id: "1",
    source: "custom.source",
    type: "com.mycompany.shop.checkout",
    dtcontext:
      'dt.session_id="234234234324235345345",dt.entity.rum_application="APPLICATION-53453458340758",host.name="123.123.123.123"',
    dataschema:
      "http://dynatrace.com/schema/bizevents/generic/1.0",
    traceparent:
      "00-4bf92f3577b34da6a3ce929d0e0e4736-00f067aa0ba902b7-00",
    data: {
      id: "OR-838475",
      paymentType: "paypal",
      plannedDeliveryDate: "01.01.2021",
      total: 234,
    },
  },
});
```

</details>



</div>

## credentialVaultClient

```js
import { credentialVaultClient } from '@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2';
```


<a name="createcredentials"></a>
### createCredentials

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>credentialVaultClient.createCredentials(config): Promise&lt;<a href="#credentialsid">CredentialsId</a>&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Creates a new credentials set.

**Required scope:** environment-api:credentials:write
**Required permission:** environment:roles:viewer

The body must not provide an ID. An ID is assigned automatically by the Dynatrace server.



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type |
| --- | --- |
|config.body<sup>*required</sup>|<a href="#credentials" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Credentials</a>| 




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[CredentialsId](#credentialsid)|201|Success. The new credentials set has been created. The response contains the ID of the set.|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Failed. The input is invalid. &#124; Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { credentialVaultClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data = await credentialVaultClient.createCredentials({
  body: {
    name: "...",
    scopes: ["APP_ENGINE"],
    type: "AWS_MONITORING_KEY_BASED",
  },
});
```

</details>



</div>


<a name="getcredentials"></a>
### getCredentials

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>credentialVaultClient.getCredentials(config): Promise&lt;<a href="#credentialsresponseelement">CredentialsResponseElement</a>&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Gets the metadata of the specified credentials set.

**Required scope:** environment-api:credentials:read
**Required permission:** environment:roles:viewer

The credentials set itself (e.g. username/certificate and password) is not included in the response.



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.id<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The Dynatrace entity ID of the required credentials set. |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[CredentialsResponseElement](#credentialsresponseelement)|200|Success. The response contains the metadata of the credentials set.|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Failed. The input is invalid. &#124; Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { credentialVaultClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data = await credentialVaultClient.getCredentials({
  id: "...",
});
```

</details>



</div>


<a name="getcredentialsdetails"></a>
### getCredentialsDetails

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>credentialVaultClient.getCredentialsDetails(config): Promise&lt;<a href="#abstractcredentialsresponseelement" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">AbstractCredentialsResponseElement</a>&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Gets the details of the specified credentials set.

**Required scope:** environment-api:credentials:read
**Required permission:** environment:roles:viewer

The credentials set including username/certificate, password or token is included in the response.



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.id<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The Dynatrace entity ID of the required credentials set. |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[AbstractCredentialsResponseElement](#abstractcredentialsresponseelement)|200|Success. The response contains the details of the credentials set.|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Failed. The input is invalid. E.g. Requested credentials with unsupported scope. Only APP_ENGINE scope is supported. &#124; Failed. Requested credentials belong to another user.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { credentialVaultClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data =
  await credentialVaultClient.getCredentialsDetails({
    id: "...",
  });
```

</details>



</div>


<a name="getcredentialsdetailslist"></a>
### getCredentialsDetailsList

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>credentialVaultClient.getCredentialsDetailsList(config): Promise&lt;<a href="#credentialsdetailslist">CredentialsDetailsList</a>&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Gets the details of the specified credentials sets.

The credentials set including username/certificate, password or token is included in the response.



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.ids|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The list of Dynatrace entity IDs of the required credentials sets (separated by commas). |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[CredentialsDetailsList](#credentialsdetailslist)|200|Success. The response contains the details of the credentials sets.|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Failed. The input is invalid. E.g. Requested credentials with unsupported scope. Only SYNTHETIC and APP_ENGINE scope are supported. &#124; Failed. Requested credentials belong to another user. &#124; Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { credentialVaultClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data =
  await credentialVaultClient.getCredentialsDetailsList();
```

</details>



</div>


<a name="listcredentials"></a>
### listCredentials

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>credentialVaultClient.listCredentials(config): Promise&lt;<a href="#credentialslist" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">CredentialsList</a>&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Lists all sets of credentials in your environment.

**Required scope:** environment-api:credentials:read
**Required permission:** environment:roles:viewer

The credentials set itself (username/certificate and password) is not included in the response.



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.name|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Filters the result by the name. When in quotation marks, whole phrase is taken. Case insensitive. |
|config.nextPageKey|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>The cursor for the next page of results. You can find it in the <strong>nextPageKey</strong> field of the previous response.</p> <p>The first page is always returned if you don't specify the <strong>nextPageKey</strong> query parameter.</p> <p>When the <strong>nextPageKey</strong> is set to obtain subsequent pages, you must omit all other query parameters.</p>  |
|config.pageSize|[number](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Number)|<p>The amount of credentials in a single response payload.</p> <p>The maximal allowed page size is 500.</p> <p>If not set, 100 is used.</p>  |
|config.scope|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Filters credentials with specified scope. |
|config.type|"CERTIFICATE" &#124; "TOKEN" &#124; "USERNAME_PASSWORD" &#124; "AWS_MONITORING_KEY_BASED" &#124; "AWS_MONITORING_ROLE_BASED" &#124; "SNMPV3"|Filters the result by the specified credentials type. |
|config.user|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Filters credentials accessible to the user (owned by the user or the ones that are accessible for all). |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[CredentialsList](#credentialslist)|200|Success|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Failed. The input is invalid. &#124; Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { credentialVaultClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data = await credentialVaultClient.listCredentials();
```

</details>



</div>


<a name="removecredentials"></a>
### removeCredentials

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>credentialVaultClient.removeCredentials(config): Promise&lt;void&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Deletes the specified credentials set

**Required scope:** environment-api:credentials:write
**Required permission:** environment:roles:viewer

Provide credential ID in the path.



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.id<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The ID of the credentials set to be deleted. |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|void|204|Success. The credentials set has been deleted. Response doesn&apos;t have a body.|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Failed. The input is invalid. &#124; Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { credentialVaultClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data = await credentialVaultClient.removeCredentials({
  id: "...",
});
```

</details>



</div>


<a name="updatecredentials"></a>
### updateCredentials

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>credentialVaultClient.updateCredentials(config): Promise&lt;void &#124; <a href="#credentialsid">CredentialsId</a>&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Updates the specified credentials set.

**Required scope:** environment-api:credentials:write
**Required permission:** environment:roles:viewer

The body must not provide an ID. The ID should be provided in the path.



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.body<sup>*required</sup>|<a href="#credentials" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Credentials</a>| |
|config.id<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The Dynatrace entity ID of the credentials set to be updated. |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[CredentialsId](#credentialsid)|201|Success. The new credentials set has been created. The response contains the ID of the set.|
|void|204|Success. The credentials set has been updated. Response doesn&apos;t have a body.|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Failed. The input is invalid. &#124; Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { credentialVaultClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data = await credentialVaultClient.updateCredentials({
  id: "...",
  body: {
    name: "...",
    scopes: ["APP_ENGINE"],
    type: "AWS_MONITORING_KEY_BASED",
  },
});
```

</details>



</div>

## davisSecurityAdvisorClient

```js
import { davisSecurityAdvisorClient } from '@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2';
```


<a name="getadviceforsecurityproblems"></a>
### getAdviceForSecurityProblems

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>davisSecurityAdvisorClient.getAdviceForSecurityProblems(config): Promise&lt;<a href="#davissecurityadvicelist">DavisSecurityAdviceList</a>&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Provides advice for security problems.

**Required scope:** environment-api:security-problems:read
**One of the following permissions is required:**
+ environment:roles:manage-security-problems
+ environment:roles:view-security-problems



You can narrow down the output by providing the management zone and pagination. If you specify a management zone, only problems originating from that zone are included to the request.



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.managementZoneFilter|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>To specify management zones, use one of the options listed below. For each option you can specify multiple comma-separated values. If several values are specified, the <strong>OR</strong> logic applies. All values are case-sensitive and must be quoted.</p> <ul> <li>Management zone ID: ids(&quot;mzId-1&quot;, &quot;mzId-2&quot;).</li> <li>Management zone names: names(&quot;mz-1&quot;, &quot;mz-2&quot;).</li> </ul> <p>You can specify several comma-separated criteria (for example, <code>names(&quot;myMz&quot;),ids(&quot;9130632296508575249&quot;)</code>).</p>  |
|config.nextPageKey|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>The cursor for the next page of results. You can find it in the <strong>nextPageKey</strong> field of the previous response.</p> <p>The first page is always returned if you don't specify the <strong>nextPageKey</strong> query parameter.</p> <p>When the <strong>nextPageKey</strong> is set to obtain subsequent pages, you must omit all other query parameters.</p>  |
|config.pageSize|[number](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Number)|<p>The amount of security advices in a single response payload.</p> <p>The maximal allowed page size is 500.</p> <p>If not set, 5 is used.</p>  |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[DavisSecurityAdviceList](#davissecurityadvicelist)|200|Success|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { davisSecurityAdvisorClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data =
  await davisSecurityAdvisorClient.getAdviceForSecurityProblems();
```

</details>



</div>

## eventsClient

```js
import { eventsClient } from '@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2';
```


<a name="createevent"></a>
### createEvent

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>eventsClient.createEvent(config): Promise&lt;<a href="#eventingestresults" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">EventIngestResults</a>&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Ingests a custom event

**One of the following scopes is required:**
+ storage:events:write
+ environment-api:events:write


**One of the following permissions is required:**
+ environment:roles:viewer
+ storage:events:write



The ingestion of custom events is subject to licensing (see [licensing documentation](https://www.dynatrace.com/support/help/shortlink/dps-events)).



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type |
| --- | --- |
|config.body<sup>*required</sup>|<a href="#eventingest">EventIngest</a>| 




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |

|[EventIngestResults](#eventingestresults)|201|The event ingest request was received by the server. The response body indicates for each event whether its creation was successful.|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { eventsClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data = await eventsClient.createEvent({
  body: { eventType: "AVAILABILITY_EVENT", title: "..." },
});
```

</details>

| Name | Type | Description |

|config.nextPageKey|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>The cursor for the next page of results. You can find it in the <strong>nextPageKey</strong> field of the previous response.</p> <p>The first page is always returned if you don't specify the <strong>nextPageKey</strong> query parameter.</p> <p>When the <strong>nextPageKey</strong> is set to obtain subsequent pages, you must omit all other query parameters.</p>  |
|config.pageSize|[number](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Number)|<p>The amount of event types in a single response payload.</p> <p>The maximal allowed page size is 500.</p> <p>If not set, 100 is used.</p>  |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[EventTypeList](#eventtypelist)|200|Success|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|
