<a name="getsettingsobjects"></a>
### getSettingsObjects

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>settingsObjectsClient.getSettingsObjects(config): Promise&lt;<a href="#objectslist" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">ObjectsList</a>&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Lists persisted settings objects

**Required scope:** settings:objects:read
**One of the following permissions is required:**
+ environment:roles:manage-settings
+ settings:objects:read



Lists persisted settings objects for selected schemas at selected scopes (or entities).

If nothing is persisted or if all persisted settings objects are not accessible due to missing permissions, no items will be returned.

**Deprecation notice:** The ability to query values of any schema by leaving out the `schemaIds` parameter in the first-page query is deprecated and will eventually be removed.

To query the effective values (including schema defaults) please see /settings/effectiveValues.



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.adminAccess|[boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Boolean)|If set to true and user has settings:objects:admin permission, the endpoint will act as if the user is the owner of all objects |
|config.externalIds|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>A list of comma-separated external IDs that the requested objects have.</p> <p>Each external ID has a maximum length of 500 characters.</p> <p>Only considered on load of the first page, when the <strong>nextPageKey</strong> is not set.</p>  |
|config.fields|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>A list of fields to be included to the response. The provided set of fields replaces the default set.</p> <p>Specify the required top-level fields, separated by commas (for example, <code>objectId,value</code>).</p> <p>Supported fields: <code>objectId</code>, <code>summary</code>, <code>searchSummary</code>, <code>created</code>, <code>modified</code>, <code>createdBy</code>, <code>modifiedBy</code>, <code>author</code>, <code>updateToken</code>, <code>scope</code>, <code>modificationInfo</code> (deprecated), <code>resourceContext</code>, <code>owner</code>, <code>schemaId</code>, <code>schemaVersion</code>, <code>value</code>, <code>externalId</code>.</p>  |
|config.filter|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>The filter parameter, as explained <a href="https://dt-url.net/platform-services-filtering" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" >here</a>.</p> <p>Filtering is supported on the following fields:</p> <ul> <li><code>created</code></li> <li><code>modified</code></li> <li><code>createdBy</code></li> <li><code>modifiedBy</code></li> <li><code>author</code> (deprecated, will not work for future schemas)</li> <li><code>value</code> with properties and sub-properties separated by dot (for example, <code>value.owningApp = 'Notebooks'</code>)</li> </ul> <p>If this parameter is omitted, all settings objects will be returned. The maximum nesting depth (via parentheses) is 5. The maximum expression length is 1024 characters.</p> <p>Note that only fields included to the response via <code>fields</code> can be used for filtering.</p>  |
|config.nextPageKey|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>The cursor for the next page of results. You can find it in the <strong>nextPageKey</strong> field of the previous response.</p> <p>The first page is always returned if you don't specify the <strong>nextPageKey</strong> query parameter.</p> <p>When the <strong>nextPageKey</strong> is set to obtain subsequent pages, you must omit all other query parameters.</p>  |
|config.pageSize|[number](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Number)|<p>The amount of settings objects in a single response payload.</p> <p>The maximal allowed page size is 500.</p> <p>If not set, 100 is used.</p>  |
|config.schemaIds|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>A list of comma-separated schema IDs to which the requested objects belong.</p> <p>To load the first page, when the <strong>nextPageKey</strong> is not set, either this parameter or <strong>scopes</strong> is required.</p> <p>To load all objects belonging to the given schema IDs leave the <strong>scopes</strong> parameter empty.</p>  |
|config.scopes|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>A list of comma-separated scopes, that the requested objects target.</p> <p>The selection only matches objects directly targeting the specified scopes. For example, <code>environment</code> will not match objects that target a host within environment. For more details, please see <a href="https://dt-url.net/ky03459" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" >Dynatrace Documentation</a>.</p> <p>To load the first page, when the <strong>nextPageKey</strong> is not set, either this parameter or <strong>schemaIds</strong> is required.</p> <p>To load all objects belonging to the given scopes leave the <strong>schemaIds</strong> parameter empty.</p>  |
|config.sort|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>The sort parameter, as explained <a href="https://dt-url.net/platform-services-filtering" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" >here</a>.</p> <p>Sorting is supported on the following fields:</p> <ul> <li><code>created</code></li> <li><code>modified</code></li> <li><code>createdBy</code></li> <li><code>modifiedBy</code></li> <li><code>author</code> (deprecated, will not work for future schemas)</li> <li><code>value</code> with properties and sub-properties separated by dot (for example, <code>value.owningApp</code>)</li> </ul> <p>Note that only fields included to the response via <code>fields</code> can be used for sorting.</p>  |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[ObjectsList](#objectslist)|200|Success. Accessible objects returned.|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Failed. Forbidden. &#124; Failed. The specified schema or scope is not found. &#124; Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { settingsObjectsClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data =
  await settingsObjectsClient.getSettingsObjects();
```

</details>



</div>


<a name="postsettingsobjects"></a>
### postSettingsObjects

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>settingsObjectsClient.postSettingsObjects(config): Promise&lt;Array&lt;<a href="#settingsobjectresponse">SettingsObjectResponse</a>&gt;&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Creates a new settings object

**Required scope:** settings:objects:write
**One of the following permissions is required:**
+ environment:roles:manage-settings
+ settings:objects:write



You can upload several objects at once. In that case each object returns its own response code. Check the response body for details.



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.adminAccess|[boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Boolean)|If set to true and user has settings:objects:admin permission, the endpoint will act as if the user is the owner of all objects |
|config.body<sup>*required</sup>|Array&lt;<a href="#settingsobjectcreate" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">SettingsObjectCreate</a>&gt;| |
|config.validateOnly|[boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Boolean)|If <code>true</code>, the request runs only validation of the submitted settings objects, without saving them. |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[SettingsObjectResponse](#settingsobjectresponse)|200|Success|
|[SettingsObjectResponse](#settingsobjectresponse)|207|Multi-status: different objects in the payload resulted in different statuses.|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|SettingsObjectResponseArrayError|Failed. Schema validation failed. &#124; Failed. The requested resource doesn't exist. &#124; Failed. Conflicting resource.|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Failed. Forbidden. &#124; Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { settingsObjectsClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data =
  await settingsObjectsClient.postSettingsObjects({
    body: [
      {
        schemaId:
          "builtin:container.built-in-monitoring-rule",
        scope: "HOST-D3A3C5A146830A79",
        value: {},
      },
    ],
  });
```

</details>



</div>


<a name="putsettingsobjectbyobjectid"></a>
### putSettingsObjectByObjectId

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>settingsObjectsClient.putSettingsObjectByObjectId(config): Promise&lt;<a href="#settingsobjectresponse">SettingsObjectResponse</a>&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Updates an existing settings object

**Required scope:** settings:objects:write
**One of the following permissions is required:**
+ environment:roles:manage-settings
+ settings:objects:write



To update a property of the `secret` type you need to pass the new value unmasked. To keep the current value, send the current masked secret. You can obtain it via [GET an object](https://dt-url.net/kj036oj) endpoint.

Some schemas don&apos;t allow passing of the masked secret. In that case you need to send the unmasked secret with every update of the object.



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.adminAccess|[boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Boolean)|If set to true and user has settings:objects:admin permission, the endpoint will act as if the user is the owner of all objects |
|config.body<sup>*required</sup>|<a href="#settingsobjectupdate" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">SettingsObjectUpdate</a>| |
|config.objectId<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The ID of the required settings object. |
|config.validateOnly|[boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Boolean)|If <code>true</code>, the request runs only validation of the submitted settings object, without saving it. |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[SettingsObjectResponse](#settingsobjectresponse)|200|Success|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|SettingsObjectResponseError|Failed. Schema validation failed. &#124; Failed. The requested resource doesn't exist. &#124; Failed. Conflicting resource.|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Failed. Forbidden. &#124; Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { settingsObjectsClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data =
  await settingsObjectsClient.putSettingsObjectByObjectId({
    objectId: "...",
    body: { value: {} },
  });
```

</details>



</div>


<a name="removepermission"></a>
### removePermission

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>settingsObjectsClient.removePermission(config): Promise&lt;void&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Remove the permissions for an existing accessor on this object.

**Required scope:** settings:objects:write
**Required permission:** settings:objects:write

Remove the permissions for an existing accessor on this object, anyone with read/write permissions is allowed to delete permissions.



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.accessorId<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The user uuid or group uuid of the accessor, depending on the type. |
|config.accessorType<sup>*required</sup>|"group" &#124; "user"|The type of the accessor. |
|config.adminAccess|[boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Boolean)|If set to true and user has settings:objects:admin permission, the endpoint will act as if the user is the owner of all objects |
|config.objectId<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The ID of the required settings object. |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|void|204|Success|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|No object available for the given objectId or the accessor doesn't have any permissions on this object. &#124; Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { settingsObjectsClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data = await settingsObjectsClient.removePermission({
  objectId: "...",
  accessorType: "group",
  accessorId: "...",
});
```

</details>



</div>


<a name="removepermissionallusers"></a>
### removePermissionAllUsers

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>settingsObjectsClient.removePermissionAllUsers(config): Promise&lt;void&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Remove the permissions for an existing all-users accessor on this object.

**Required scope:** settings:objects:write
**Required permission:** settings:objects:write

Remove the permissions for an existing all-users accessor on this object, anyone with read/write permissions is allowed to delete permissions.



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.adminAccess|[boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Boolean)|If set to true and user has settings:objects:admin permission, the endpoint will act as if the user is the owner of all objects |
|config.objectId<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The ID of the required settings object. |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|void|204|Success|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|No object available for the given objectId or the all-users accessor doesn't have any permissions on this object. &#124; Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { settingsObjectsClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data =
  await settingsObjectsClient.removePermissionAllUsers({
    objectId: "...",
  });
```

</details>



</div>


<a name="resolveeffectivepermissions"></a>
### resolveEffectivePermissions

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>settingsObjectsClient.resolveEffectivePermissions(config): Promise&lt;Array&lt;<a href="#effectivepermission">EffectivePermission</a>&gt;&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Get the effective settings permissions of the calling user in the environment

**One of the following scopes is required:**
+ settings:objects:write
+ settings:objects:read
+ settings:schemas:read


**One of the following permissions is required:**
+ environment:roles:viewer
+ settings:schemas:read



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type |
| --- | --- |
|config.body<sup>*required</sup>|<a href="#resolutionrequest" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">ResolutionRequest</a>| 




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[EffectivePermission](#effectivepermission)|200|Success|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Failed. Forbidden. No access to any settings. &#124; Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { settingsObjectsClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data =
  await settingsObjectsClient.resolveEffectivePermissions({
    body: {
      permissions: [
        {
          context: {
            schemaId: "service:schema",
            scope: "environment",
          },
          permission: "settings:objects:write",
        },
      ],
    },
  });
```

</details>



</div>


<a name="transferownership"></a>
### transferOwnership

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>settingsObjectsClient.transferOwnership(config): Promise&lt;void&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Transfer ownership of the object.

**Required scope:** settings:objects:write
**Required permission:** settings:objects:write

Transfer ownership of the object, only the owner or the main admin is allowed to transfer the ownership (IAM permission needed).



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.adminAccess|[boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Boolean)|If set to true and user has settings:objects:admin permission, the endpoint will act as if the user is the owner of all objects |
|config.body<sup>*required</sup>|<a href="#transferownershiprequest">TransferOwnershipRequest</a>| |
|config.objectId<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The ID of the required settings object. |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|void|204|Success. Response doesn&apos;t have a body.|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Failed. The input is invalid. &#124; Failed. Forbidden. &#124; No object available for the given objectId. &#124; Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { settingsObjectsClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data = await settingsObjectsClient.transferOwnership({
  objectId: "...",
  body: { newOwner: { type: "user" } },
});
```

</details>



</div>

**Required permission:** settings:objects:write

```ts
  accessorType: "group",

});
```

```js
import { settingsSchemasClient } from '@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2';
```

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>settingsSchemasClient.getAvailableSchemaDefinitions(config): Promise&lt;<a href="#schemalist" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">SchemaList</a>&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Lists available settings schemas

**Required scope:** settings:schemas:read
**One of the following permissions is required:**
+ environment:roles:manage-settings
+ settings:schemas:read

#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.fields|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>A list of fields to be included to the response. The provided set of fields replaces the default set.</p> <p>Specify the required top-level fields, separated by commas (for example, <code>schemaId,displayName</code>).</p> <p>Supported fields: <code>schemaId</code>, <code>displayName</code>, <code>maturity</code>, <code>latestSchemaVersion</code>, <code>multiObject</code>, <code>ordered</code>, <code>ownerBasedAccessControl</code>.</p>  |

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[SchemaList](#schemalist)|200|Success|

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { settingsSchemasClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

  await settingsSchemasClient.getAvailableSchemaDefinitions();
```

</details>

```js
import { syntheticHttpMonitorExecutionsClient } from '@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2';
```

<a name="getexecutionresult"></a>
### getExecutionResult

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>syntheticHttpMonitorExecutionsClient.getExecutionResult(config): Promise&lt;<a href="#monitorexecutionresults" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">MonitorExecutionResults</a>&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Gets detailed information about the last execution of the specified HTTP monitor

**Required scope:** environment-api:synthetic-execution:read
**Required permission:** environment:roles:viewer

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |

|config.monitorId<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Identifier of the HTTP monitor for which last execution result is returned. |
|config.resultType<sup>*required</sup>|"SUCCESS" &#124; "FAILED"|Defines the result type of the last HTTP monitor's execution. |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[MonitorExecutionResults](#monitorexecutionresults)|200|Success. The response contains detailed data.|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { syntheticHttpMonitorExecutionsClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data =
  await syntheticHttpMonitorExecutionsClient.getExecutionResult(
    { monitorId: "...", resultType: "SUCCESS" },
  );
```

</details>

```js
import { syntheticLocationsNodesAndConfigurationClient } from '@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2';
```

```ts
import { syntheticLocationsNodesAndConfigurationClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";
```

<a name="getlocationdeploymentapplycommands"></a>
### getLocationDeploymentApplyCommands

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>syntheticLocationsNodesAndConfigurationClient.getLocationDeploymentApplyCommands(config): Promise&lt;[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Gets list of commands to deploy synthetic location in Kubernetes/Openshift cluster

**Required scope:** environment-api:synthetic:read
**Required permission:** environment:roles:manage-settings



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.filename|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Filename |
|config.fipsMode|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Fips mode, accepted values are: DISABLED, ENABLED, ENABLED_WITH_CORPORATE_PROXY. Default value is DISABLED. |
|config.namespace|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Namespace |
|config.platform|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Container platform, currently supported are: KUBERNETES and OPENSHIFT. Default value is KUBERNETES. |
|config.squidProxyConfig|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Optional configuration for Squid corporate proxy. If not provided the default value will be used. |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|void|200|Success. The response contains the list of commands that needs to be executed to deploy a synthetic location.|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { syntheticLocationsNodesAndConfigurationClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data =
  await syntheticLocationsNodesAndConfigurationClient.getLocationDeploymentApplyCommands();
```

</details>



</div>

<a name="getlocationdeploymentyaml"></a>
### getLocationDeploymentYaml

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>syntheticLocationsNodesAndConfigurationClient.getLocationDeploymentYaml(config): Promise&lt;Binary&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Gets yaml file content to deploy location in Kubernetes/Openshift cluster

**Required scope:** environment-api:synthetic:read
**Required permission:** environment:roles:manage-settings



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.activeGateName|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Active gate name |
|config.customRegistry|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Custom images registry prefix - this will replace 'dynatrace' in image URLs in generated yaml. |
|config.locationId<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The Dynatrace entity ID of the required location. |
|config.namespace|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Namespace |
|config.tagVersionActiveGate|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Custom version tag for Active Gate - this will be used as desired Active Gate version in generated yaml. |
|config.tagVersionSynthetic|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Custom version tag for Synthetic- this will be used as desired Synthetic version in generated yaml |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|void|200|Success. The response contains the content of deployment yaml file.|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|

<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
  await syntheticLocationsNodesAndConfigurationClient.getLocationDeploymentYaml(
    { locationId: "..." },
  );
```

</details>

<a name="getlocations"></a>

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>syntheticLocationsNodesAndConfigurationClient.getLocations(config): Promise&lt;<a href="#syntheticlocations" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">SyntheticLocations</a>&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Lists all synthetic locations (both public and private) available for your environment

**Required scope:** environment-api:synthetic:read
**Required permission:** environment:roles:viewer



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.capability|"BROWSER" &#124; "HTTP" &#124; "HTTP_HIGH_RESOURCE" &#124; "ICMP" &#124; "TCP" &#124; "DNS"|Filters the resulting set of locations to those which support specific capability. |
|config.cloudPlatform|"AWS" &#124; "AZURE" &#124; "ALIBABA" &#124; "GOOGLE_CLOUD" &#124; "OTHER"|Filters the resulting set of locations to those which are hosted on a specific cloud platform. |
|config.type|"PUBLIC" &#124; "PRIVATE"|Filters the resulting set of locations to those of a specific type. |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[SyntheticLocations](#syntheticlocations)|200|Success|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts

  await syntheticLocationsNodesAndConfigurationClient.getLocations();
```

</details>

|---|---|---|

|---|---|

<summary>
Code example
</summary>

<a name="getmonitors"></a>
### getMonitors

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>syntheticNetworkAvailabilityMonitorsClient.getMonitors(config): Promise&lt;<a href="#syntheticmonitorlistdto">SyntheticMonitorListDto</a>&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Gets all synthetic monitors. Currently browser and network availability monitors only. | maturity=EARLY_ADOPTER

**Required scope:** environment-api:synthetic-monitors:read
**Required permission:** environment:roles:manage-settings



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.monitorSelector|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>Defines the scope of the query. Only monitors matching the specified criteria are included into response.</p> <p>You can add one or several of the criteria listed below. For each criterion you can specify multiple comma-separated values, unless stated otherwise. If several values are specified, the <strong>OR</strong> logic applies.</p> <ul> <li>Monitor type: <code>type(HTTP,MULTI_PROTOCOL)</code>. Possible values: 'HTTP', 'BROWSER', 'THIRD_PARTY', 'MULTI_PROTOCOL' (Note that only 'BROWSER' and 'MULTI_PROTOCOL' are currently supported).</li> <li>Management zone ID: <code>managementZoneId(1, 2)</code>.</li> <li>Synthetic Location ME ID: <code>location(SYNTHETIC_LOCATION-123)</code>.</li> <li>Monitored host ME ID: <code>monitoredEntity(HOST-123)</code>.</li> <li>Monitor tags: <code>tag(\[context]key:value,key:value,key)</code>. Tags in <code>\[context]key:value</code>, <code>key:value</code>, and <code>key</code> formats are detected and parsed automatically. If a key-only tag has a colon (<code>:</code>) in it, you must escape the colon with a backslash(<code>\\</code>). Otherwise, the tag will be parsed as a <code>key:value tag</code>. All tag values are case-sensitive.</li> <li>Monitor enablement: <code>enabled(true)</code>.</li> </ul> <p>To set several criteria, separate them with a comma (<code>,</code>). Only results matching <strong>all</strong> criteria are included in the response.</p>  |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[SyntheticMonitorListDto](#syntheticmonitorlistdto)|200|Success|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { syntheticNetworkAvailabilityMonitorsClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data =
  await syntheticNetworkAvailabilityMonitorsClient.getMonitors();
```

</details>



</div>


<a name="updatemonitor"></a>
### updateMonitor

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>syntheticNetworkAvailabilityMonitorsClient.updateMonitor(config): Promise&lt;void&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Updates a synthetic monitor definition for the given monitor ID. Currently browser and network availability monitors only. | maturity=EARLY_ADOPTER

**Required scope:** environment-api:synthetic-monitors:write
**Required permission:** environment:roles:manage-settings



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.body<sup>*required</sup>|<a href="#syntheticbrowsermonitorrequest" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">SyntheticBrowserMonitorRequest</a> &#124; <a href="#syntheticmultiprotocolmonitorrequest">SyntheticMultiProtocolMonitorRequest</a>| |
|config.monitorId<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The identifier of the monitor. |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|void|204|Success. Response doesn&apos;t have a body.|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { syntheticNetworkAvailabilityMonitorsClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data =
  await syntheticNetworkAvailabilityMonitorsClient.updateMonitor(
    { monitorId: "..." },
  );
```

</details>



</div>

## syntheticOnDemandMonitorExecutionsClient

```js
import { syntheticOnDemandMonitorExecutionsClient } from '@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2';
```


<a name="execute"></a>
### execute

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>syntheticOnDemandMonitorExecutionsClient.execute(config): Promise&lt;<a href="#syntheticondemandexecutionresult" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">SyntheticOnDemandExecutionResult</a>&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Triggers on-demand executions for synthetic monitors

**Required scope:** environment-api:synthetic-execution:write
**Required permission:** environment:roles:viewer



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type |
| --- | --- |
|config.body<sup>*required</sup>|<a href="#syntheticondemandexecutionrequest">SyntheticOnDemandExecutionRequest</a>| 




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[SyntheticOnDemandExecutionResult](#syntheticondemandexecutionresult)|201|Success. The monitor&apos;s execution response details|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Failed. The input is invalid. &#124; Unavailable &#124; Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { syntheticOnDemandMonitorExecutionsClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data =
  await syntheticOnDemandMonitorExecutionsClient.execute({
    body: {},
  });
```

</details>



</div>


<a name="getbatch"></a>
### getBatch

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>syntheticOnDemandMonitorExecutionsClient.getBatch(config): Promise&lt;<a href="#syntheticondemandbatchstatus" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">SyntheticOnDemandBatchStatus</a>&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Gets summary information and the list of failed executions for the given batch ID

**Required scope:** environment-api:synthetic-execution:read
**Required permission:** environment:roles:viewer



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.batchId<sup>*required</sup>|[number](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Number)|The batch identifier of the executions. |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[SyntheticOnDemandBatchStatus](#syntheticondemandbatchstatus)|200|Success|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Failed. The input is invalid. &#124; Batch with the given ID doesn't exist. &#124; Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { syntheticOnDemandMonitorExecutionsClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data =
  await syntheticOnDemandMonitorExecutionsClient.getBatch({
    batchId: 10,
  });
```

</details>



</div>


<a name="getexecution"></a>
### getExecution

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>syntheticOnDemandMonitorExecutionsClient.getExecution(config): Promise&lt;<a href="#syntheticondemandexecution">SyntheticOnDemandExecution</a>&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Gets basic results of the specified on-demand execution

**Required scope:** environment-api:synthetic-execution:read
**Required permission:** environment:roles:viewer



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.executionId<sup>*required</sup>|[number](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Number)|The identifier of the on-demand execution. |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[SyntheticOnDemandExecution](#syntheticondemandexecution)|200|Success. The response contains basic information about the on-demand execution.|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Failed. The input is invalid. &#124; Execution with the given ID doesn't exist. &#124; Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { syntheticOnDemandMonitorExecutionsClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data =
  await syntheticOnDemandMonitorExecutionsClient.getExecution(
    { executionId: 10 },
  );
```

</details>



</div>

<a name="getexecutions"></a>
### getExecutions

<div class="padding-bottom--md">
<strong>syntheticOnDemandMonitorExecutionsClient.getExecutions(config): Promise&lt;<a href="#syntheticondemandexecutions">SyntheticOnDemandExecutions</a>&gt;</strong>

<div class="padding-left--md">

Gets the list of all on-demand executions of synthetic monitors

**Required scope:** environment-api:synthetic-execution:read
**Required permission:** environment:roles:viewer



</div>


#### Parameters

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|config.batchId|[number](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Number)|Filters the resulting set of the executions by batch. Specify the ID of the batch. |
|config.dataDeliveryFrom|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>The start of the requested timeframe for data delivering timestamps.</p> <p>You can use one of the following formats:</p> <ul> <li>Timestamp in UTC milliseconds.</li> <li>Human-readable format of <code>2021-01-25T05:57:01.123+01:00</code>. If no time zone is specified, UTC is used. You can use a space character instead of the <code>T</code>. Seconds and fractions of a second are optional.</li> <li>Relative timeframe, back from now. The format is <code>now-NU/A</code>, where <code>N</code> is the amount of time, <code>U</code> is the unit of time, and <code>A</code> is an alignment. The alignment rounds all the smaller values to the nearest zero in the past. For example, <code>now-1y/w</code> is one year back, aligned by a week. You can also specify relative timeframe without an alignment: <code>now-NU</code>. Supported time units for the relative timeframe are: <ul> <li><code>m</code>: minutes</li> <li><code>h</code>: hours</li> <li><code>d</code>: days</li> <li><code>w</code>: weeks</li> <li><code>M</code>: months</li> <li><code>y</code>: years</li> </ul> </li> </ul> <p>If not set, the maximum relative timeframe of six hours is used (<code>now-6h</code>).</p>  |
|config.dataDeliveryTo|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>The end of the requested timeframe for data delivering timestamps.</p> <p>You can use one of the following formats:</p> <ul> <li>Timestamp in UTC milliseconds.</li> <li>Human-readable format of <code>2021-01-25T05:57:01.123+01:00</code>. If no time zone is specified, UTC is used. You can use a space character instead of the <code>T</code>. Seconds and fractions of a second are optional.</li> <li>Relative timeframe, back from now. The format is <code>now-NU/A</code>, where <code>N</code> is the amount of time, <code>U</code> is the unit of time, and <code>A</code> is an alignment. The alignment rounds all the smaller values to the nearest zero in the past. For example, <code>now-1y/w</code> is one year back, aligned by a week. You can also specify relative timeframe without an alignment: <code>now-NU</code>. Supported time units for the relative timeframe are: <ul> <li><code>m</code>: minutes</li> <li><code>h</code>: hours</li> <li><code>d</code>: days</li> <li><code>w</code>: weeks</li> <li><code>M</code>: months</li> <li><code>y</code>: years</li> </ul> </li> </ul> <p>If not set, the current timestamp is used.</p>  |
|config.executionFrom|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>The start of the requested timeframe for execution timestamps.</p> <p>You can use one of the following formats:</p> <ul> <li>Timestamp in UTC milliseconds.</li> <li>Human-readable format of <code>2021-01-25T05:57:01.123+01:00</code>. If no time zone is specified, UTC is used. You can use a space character instead of the <code>T</code>. Seconds and fractions of a second are optional.</li> <li>Relative timeframe, back from now. The format is <code>now-NU/A</code>, where <code>N</code> is the amount of time, <code>U</code> is the unit of time, and <code>A</code> is an alignment. The alignment rounds all the smaller values to the nearest zero in the past. For example, <code>now-1y/w</code> is one year back, aligned by a week. You can also specify relative timeframe without an alignment: <code>now-NU</code>. Supported time units for the relative timeframe are: <ul> <li><code>m</code>: minutes</li> <li><code>h</code>: hours</li> <li><code>d</code>: days</li> <li><code>w</code>: weeks</li> <li><code>M</code>: months</li> <li><code>y</code>: years</li> </ul> </li> </ul> <p>If not set, the maximum relative timeframe of six hours is used (<code>now-6h</code>).</p>  |
|config.executionStage|"TRIGGERED" &#124; "EXECUTED" &#124; "DATA_RETRIEVED"|Filters the resulting set of executions by their stage. |
|config.executionTo|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>The end of the requested timeframe for execution timestamps.</p> <p>You can use one of the following formats:</p> <ul> <li>Timestamp in UTC milliseconds.</li> <li>Human-readable format of <code>2021-01-25T05:57:01.123+01:00</code>. If no time zone is specified, UTC is used. You can use a space character instead of the <code>T</code>. Seconds and fractions of a second are optional.</li> <li>Relative timeframe, back from now. The format is <code>now-NU/A</code>, where <code>N</code> is the amount of time, <code>U</code> is the unit of time, and <code>A</code> is an alignment. The alignment rounds all the smaller values to the nearest zero in the past. For example, <code>now-1y/w</code> is one year back, aligned by a week. You can also specify relative timeframe without an alignment: <code>now-NU</code>. Supported time units for the relative timeframe are: <ul> <li><code>m</code>: minutes</li> <li><code>h</code>: hours</li> <li><code>d</code>: days</li> <li><code>w</code>: weeks</li> <li><code>M</code>: months</li> <li><code>y</code>: years</li> </ul> </li> </ul> <p>If not set, the current timestamp is used.</p>  |
|config.locationId|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Filters the resulting set of the executions by Synthetic location. Specify the ID of the location. |
|config.monitorId|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Filters the resulting set of the executions by monitor synthetic monitor. Specify the ID of the monitor. |
|config.schedulingFrom|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>The start of the requested timeframe for scheduling timestamps.</p> <p>You can use one of the following formats:</p> <ul> <li>Timestamp in UTC milliseconds.</li> <li>Human-readable format of <code>2021-01-25T05:57:01.123+01:00</code>. If no time zone is specified, UTC is used. You can use a space character instead of the <code>T</code>. Seconds and fractions of a second are optional.</li> <li>Relative timeframe, back from now. The format is <code>now-NU/A</code>, where <code>N</code> is the amount of time, <code>U</code> is the unit of time, and <code>A</code> is an alignment. The alignment rounds all the smaller values to the nearest zero in the past. For example, <code>now-1y/w</code> is one year back, aligned by a week. You can also specify relative timeframe without an alignment: <code>now-NU</code>. Supported time units for the relative timeframe are: <ul> <li><code>m</code>: minutes</li> <li><code>h</code>: hours</li> <li><code>d</code>: days</li> <li><code>w</code>: weeks</li> <li><code>M</code>: months</li> <li><code>y</code>: years</li> </ul> </li> </ul> <p>If not set, the maximum relative timeframe of six hours is used (<code>now-6h</code>).</p>  |
|config.schedulingTo|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>The end of the requested timeframe for scheduling timestamps.</p> <p>You can use one of the following formats:</p> <ul> <li>Timestamp in UTC milliseconds.</li> <li>Human-readable format of <code>2021-01-25T05:57:01.123+01:00</code>. If no time zone is specified, UTC is used. You can use a space character instead of the <code>T</code>. Seconds and fractions of a second are optional.</li> <li>Relative timeframe, back from now. The format is <code>now-NU/A</code>, where <code>N</code> is the amount of time, <code>U</code> is the unit of time, and <code>A</code> is an alignment. The alignment rounds all the smaller values to the nearest zero in the past. For example, <code>now-1y/w</code> is one year back, aligned by a week. You can also specify relative timeframe without an alignment: <code>now-NU</code>. Supported time units for the relative timeframe are: <ul> <li><code>m</code>: minutes</li> <li><code>h</code>: hours</li> <li><code>d</code>: days</li> <li><code>w</code>: weeks</li> <li><code>M</code>: months</li> <li><code>y</code>: years</li> </ul> </li> </ul> <p>If not set, the current timestamp is used.</p>  |
|config.source|"API" &#124; "UI"|Filters the resulting set of the executions by the source of the triggering request. |
|config.userId|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Filters the resulting set of executions by scheduled user. |




#### Returns

| Return type | Status code | Description |
|---|---|---|
|[SyntheticOnDemandExecutions](#syntheticondemandexecutions)|200|Success|


#### Throws

| Error Type | Error Message |
|---|---|
|ErrorEnvelopeError|Failed. The input is invalid. &#124; Client side error. &#124; Server side error.|


<details>
<summary>
Code example
</summary>

```ts
import { syntheticOnDemandMonitorExecutionsClient } from "@dynatrace-sdk/client-classic-environment-v2";

const data =
  await syntheticOnDemandMonitorExecutionsClient.getExecutions();
```

</details>



</div>

## Types

### AWSKeyBasedCredentialsDto

A credentials set of the `AWS_MONITORING_KEY_BASED` type.

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|accessKeyID<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Access Key ID  of the credentials set. |
|allowContextlessRequests|[boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Boolean)|Allow ad-hoc functions to access the credential details (requires the APP_ENGINE scope). |
|allowedEntities|Array&lt;<a href="#credentialaccessdata">CredentialAccessData</a>&gt;|The set of entities allowed to use the credential. |
|awsPartition<sup>*required</sup>|"CHINA" &#124; "DEFAULT" &#124; "US_GOV"|AWS partition of the credential. |
|description|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|A short description of the credentials set. |
|id|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The ID of the credentials set. |
|name<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The name of the credentials set. |
|ownerAccessOnly|[boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Boolean)|The credentials set is available to every user (<code>false</code>) or to owner only (<code>true</code>). |
|~~scope~~<sup>**DEPRECATED**</sup>|"EXTENSION" &#124; "SYNTHETIC" &#124; "APP_ENGINE"|The scope of the credentials set. |
|scopes<sup>*required</sup>|Array&lt;"EXTENSION" &#124; "SYNTHETIC" &#124; "APP_ENGINE"&gt;|<p>The set of scopes of the credentials set.</p> <p>Limitations: <code>CredentialsScope.APP_ENGINE</code> is only available on the new Dynatrace SaaS platform - it's not available on managed or non-Grail SaaS environments.</p>  |
|secretKey<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Secret access key of the credential. |
|type<sup>*required</sup>|"CERTIFICATE" &#124; "PUBLIC_CERTIFICATE" &#124; "TOKEN" &#124; "USERNAME_PASSWORD" &#124; "AWS_MONITORING_KEY_BASED" &#124; "AWS_MONITORING_ROLE_BASED" &#124; "SNMPV3"|<p>Defines the actual set of fields depending on the value. See one of the following objects:</p> <ul> <li><code>CERTIFICATE</code> -&gt; CertificateCredentials</li> <li><code>PUBLIC_CERTIFICATE</code> -&gt; PublicCertificateCredentials</li> <li><code>USERNAME_PASSWORD</code> -&gt; UserPasswordCredentials</li> <li><code>TOKEN</code> -&gt; TokenCredentials</li> <li><code>SNMPV3</code> -&gt; SNMPV3Credentials</li> <li><code>AWS_MONITORING_KEY_BASED</code> -&gt; AWSKeyBasedCredentialsDto</li> <li><code>AWS_MONITORING_ROLE_BASED</code> -&gt; AWSRoleBasedCredentials</li> </ul>  |

### AWSRoleBasedCredentials

A credentials set of the `AWS_MONITORING_ROLE_BASED` type.

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|accountID<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Amazon account ID of the credential. |
|allowContextlessRequests|[boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Boolean)|Allow ad-hoc functions to access the credential details (requires the APP_ENGINE scope). |
|allowedEntities|Array&lt;<a href="#credentialaccessdata" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">CredentialAccessData</a>&gt;|The set of entities allowed to use the credential. |
|description|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|A short description of the credentials set. |
|iamRole<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The IAM role name of the credentials set. |
|id|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The ID of the credentials set. |
|name<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The name of the credentials set. |
|ownerAccessOnly|[boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Boolean)|The credentials set is available to every user (<code>false</code>) or to owner only (<code>true</code>). |
|~~scope~~<sup>**DEPRECATED**</sup>|"EXTENSION" &#124; "SYNTHETIC" &#124; "APP_ENGINE"|The scope of the credentials set. |
|scopes<sup>*required</sup>|Array&lt;"EXTENSION" &#124; "SYNTHETIC" &#124; "APP_ENGINE"&gt;|<p>The set of scopes of the credentials set.</p> <p>Limitations: <code>CredentialsScope.APP_ENGINE</code> is only available on the new Dynatrace SaaS platform - it's not available on managed or non-Grail SaaS environments.</p>  |
|type<sup>*required</sup>|"CERTIFICATE" &#124; "PUBLIC_CERTIFICATE" &#124; "TOKEN" &#124; "USERNAME_PASSWORD" &#124; "AWS_MONITORING_KEY_BASED" &#124; "AWS_MONITORING_ROLE_BASED" &#124; "SNMPV3"|<p>Defines the actual set of fields depending on the value. See one of the following objects:</p> <ul> <li><code>CERTIFICATE</code> -&gt; CertificateCredentials</li> <li><code>PUBLIC_CERTIFICATE</code> -&gt; PublicCertificateCredentials</li> <li><code>USERNAME_PASSWORD</code> -&gt; UserPasswordCredentials</li> <li><code>TOKEN</code> -&gt; TokenCredentials</li> <li><code>SNMPV3</code> -&gt; SNMPV3Credentials</li> <li><code>AWS_MONITORING_KEY_BASED</code> -&gt; AWSKeyBasedCredentialsDto</li> <li><code>AWS_MONITORING_ROLE_BASED</code> -&gt; AWSRoleBasedCredentials</li> </ul>  |

### AbstractCredentialsResponseElement

Credentials set.

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|credentialUsageSummary<sup>*required</sup>|Array&lt;<a href="#credentialusagehandler">CredentialUsageHandler</a>&gt;|The list contains summary data related to the use of credentials. |
|description<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|A short description of the credentials set. |
|externalVault|<a href="#externalvaultconfig" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">ExternalVaultConfig</a>|Configuration for external vault synchronization for username and password credentials. |
|id|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The ID of the credentials set. |
|name<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The name of the credentials set. |
|owner<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The owner of the credential (user for which used API token was created). |
|ownerAccessOnly<sup>*required</sup>|[boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Boolean)|Flag indicating that this credential is visible only to the owner. |
|scope|"ALL" &#124; "EXTENSION" &#124; "SYNTHETIC" &#124; "UNKNOWN"|The scope of the credentials set. |
|type<sup>*required</sup>|"CERTIFICATE" &#124; "PUBLIC_CERTIFICATE" &#124; "TOKEN" &#124; "USERNAME_PASSWORD"|<p>Defines the actual set of fields depending on the value. See one of the following objects:</p> <ul> <li><code>USERNAME_PASSWORD</code> -&gt; CredentialsDetailsUsernamePasswordResponseElement</li> <li><code>CERTIFICATE</code> -&gt; CredentialsDetailsCertificateResponseElement</li> <li><code>TOKEN</code> -&gt; CredentialsDetailsTokenResponseElement</li> <li><code>PUBLIC_CERTIFICATE</code> -&gt; CredentialsDetailsCertificateResponseElement</li> </ul>  |

### AbstractSloAlertDto

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|alertName<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Name of the alert. |
|alertThreshold<sup>*required</sup>|[number](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Number)|Threshold of the alert. Status alerts trigger if they fall below this value, burn rate alerts trigger if they exceed the value. |
|alertType<sup>*required</sup>|"BURN_RATE" &#124; "STATUS"|<p>Defines the actual set of fields depending on the value. See one of the following objects:</p> <ul> <li><code>BURN_RATE</code> -&gt; BurnRateAlert</li> <li><code>STATUS</code> -&gt; StatusAlert</li> </ul>  |

### AccessorPermissions

An accessor identity and it&apos;s associated permissions.

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|accessor|<a href="#identity">Identity</a>|An Identity describing either a user, a group, or the all-users group (applying to all users). |
|permissions|Array&lt;"r" &#124; "w"&gt;|The permissions associated with the accessor. |

### AccessorPermissionsList

All configured permissions of an object (excluding owner).

| Name | Type |
| --- | --- |
|accessors|Array&lt;<a href="#accessorpermissions" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">AccessorPermissions</a>&gt;|

### ActiveGate

Parameters of the ActiveGate.

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|activeGateTokens|Array&lt;<a href="#activegatetokeninfodto">ActiveGateTokenInfoDto</a>&gt;|A list of the ActiveGate tokens. |
|autoUpdateSettings|<a href="#activegateautoupdateconfig" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">ActiveGateAutoUpdateConfig</a>|Configuration of the ActiveGate auto-updates. |
|autoUpdateStatus|"UNKNOWN" &#124; "INCOMPATIBLE" &#124; "OUTDATED" &#124; "SCHEDULED" &#124; "SUPPRESSED" &#124; "UP2DATE" &#124; "UPDATE_IN_PROGRESS" &#124; "UPDATE_PENDING" &#124; "UPDATE_PROBLEM"|The current status of auto-updates of the ActiveGate. |
|connectedHosts|<a href="#activegateconnectedhosts">ActiveGateConnectedHosts</a>|Information about hosts currently connected to the ActiveGate |
|containerized|[boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Boolean)|ActiveGate is deployed in container (<code>true</code>) or not (<code>false</code>). |
|environments|Array&lt;[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)&gt;|A list of environments (specified by IDs) the ActiveGate can connect to. |
|fipsMode|[boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Boolean)|ActiveGate is running in FIPS compliant mode (<code>true</code>) or not (<code>false</code>). |
|group|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The group of the ActiveGate. |
|hostname|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The name of the host the ActiveGate is running on. |
|id|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The ID of the ActiveGate. |
|loadBalancerAddresses|Array&lt;[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)&gt;|A list of Load Balancer addresses of the ActiveGate. |
|mainEnvironment|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The ID of the main environment for a multi-environment ActiveGate. |
|modules|Array&lt;<a href="#activegatemodule" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">ActiveGateModule</a>&gt;|A list of modules of the ActiveGate. |
|networkAddresses|Array&lt;[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)&gt;|A list of network addresses of the ActiveGate. |
|networkZone|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The network zone of the ActiveGate. |
|offlineSince|[number](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Number)|<p>The timestamp since when the ActiveGate is offline.</p> <p>The <code>null</code> value means the ActiveGate is online.</p>  |
|osArchitecture|"S390" &#124; "X86" &#124; "ARM" &#124; "PPCLE"|The OS architecture that the ActiveGate is running on. |
|osBitness|"64"|The OS bitness that the ActiveGate is running on. |
|osType|"LINUX" &#124; "WINDOWS"|The OS type that the ActiveGate is running on. |
|type|"CLUSTER" &#124; "ENVIRONMENT" &#124; "ENVIRONMENT_MULTI"|The type of the ActiveGate. |
|version|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The current version of the ActiveGate in the <code>&lt;major&gt;.&lt;minor&gt;.&lt;revision&gt;.&lt;timestamp&gt;</code> format. |

### ActiveGateAutoUpdateConfig

Configuration of the ActiveGate auto-updates.

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|effectiveSetting|"ENABLED" &#124; "DISABLED"|<p>The actual state of the ActiveGate auto-update.</p> <p>Applicable only if the <strong>setting</strong> parameter is set to <code>INHERITED</code>. In that case, the value is taken from the parent setting. Otherwise, it's just a duplicate of the <strong>setting</strong> value.</p>  |
|setting<sup>*required</sup>|"ENABLED" &#124; "DISABLED" &#124; "INHERITED"|<p>The state of the ActiveGate auto-update: enabled, disabled, or inherited.</p> <p>If set to <code>INHERITED</code>, the setting is inherited from the global configuration set on the environment or Managed cluster level.</p>  |

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|globalSetting<sup>*required</sup>|"ENABLED" &#124; "DISABLED"|<p>The state of auto-updates for all ActiveGates connected to the environment or Managed cluster.</p> <p>This setting is inherited by all ActiveGates that have the <code>INHERITED</code> setting.</p>  |
|metadata|<a href="#configurationmetadata">ConfigurationMetadata</a>|Metadata useful for debugging |

### ActiveGateToken

Metadata of an ActiveGate token.

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|activeGateType<sup>*required</sup>|"CLUSTER" &#124; "ENVIRONMENT"|The type of the ActiveGate for which the token is valid. |
|creationDate<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The token creation date in ISO 8601 format (<code>yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSS'Z'</code>). |
|expirationDate|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>The token expiration date in ISO 8601 format (<code>yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSS'Z'</code>).</p> <p>If not set, the token never expires.</p>  |
|id<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The ActiveGate token identifier, consisting of <a href="https://dt-url.net/rn00tjg" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" >prefix and public part</a> of the token. |
|lastUsedDate|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The token last used date in ISO 8601 format (<code>yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSS'Z'</code>). |
|name<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The name of the token. |
|owner<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The owner of the token. |
|seedToken|[boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Boolean)|The token is a seed token (<code>true</code>) or an individual token (<code>false</code>). |

### ActiveGateTokenCreate

Parameters of a new ActiveGate token.

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|activeGateType<sup>*required</sup>|"CLUSTER" &#124; "ENVIRONMENT"|The type of the ActiveGate for which the token is valid. |
|expirationDate|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>The expiration date of the token.</p> <p>You can use one of the following formats:</p> <ul> <li>Timestamp in UTC milliseconds.</li> <li>Human-readable format of <code>2021-01-25T05:57:01.123+01:00</code>. If no time zone is specified, UTC is used. You can use a space character instead of the <code>T</code>. Seconds and fractions of a second are optional.</li> <li>Relative timeframe, back from now. The format is <code>now-NU/A</code>, where <code>N</code> is the amount of time, <code>U</code> is the unit of time, and <code>A</code> is an alignment. The alignment rounds all the smaller values to the nearest zero in the past. For example, <code>now-1y/w</code> is one year back, aligned by a week. You can also specify relative timeframe without an alignment: <code>now-NU</code>. Supported time units for the relative timeframe are: <ul> <li><code>m</code>: minutes</li> <li><code>h</code>: hours</li> <li><code>d</code>: days</li> <li><code>w</code>: weeks</li> <li><code>M</code>: months</li> <li><code>y</code>: years</li> </ul> </li> </ul> <p>If not set, the token never expires.</p> <p>Ensure that it is not set in the past and does not exceed <code>2 years</code> from the moment of creation.&quot;</p>  |
|name<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The name of the token. |
|seedToken|[boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Boolean)|<p>The token is a seed token (<code>true</code>) or an individual token (<code>false</code>).</p> <p>We recommend the individual token option (false).</p>  |

### ActiveGateTokenCreated

The newly created ActiveGate token.

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|expirationDate|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The token expiration date in ISO 8601 format (<code>yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSS'Z'</code>). |
|id<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The ActiveGate token identifier, consisting of <a href="https://dt-url.net/rn00tjg" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" >prefix and public part</a> of the token. |
|token<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The secret of the token. |

|environmentId|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>The environment ID to which the token belongs.</p> <p>Only available if more than one environment is supported.</p>  |
|id|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The ActiveGate token identifier, consisting of <a href="https://dt-url.net/rn00tjg" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" >prefix and public part</a> of the token. |
|state|"UNKNOWN" &#124; "ABSENT" &#124; "EXPIRING" &#124; "INVALID" &#124; "UNSUPPORTED" &#124; "VALID"|State of the ActiveGate token. |

Metadata of an API token.

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|additionalMetadata|ApiTokenAdditionalMetadata|<p>Contains additional properties for specific kinds of token. Examples:</p> <ul> <li>A <code>dashboardId</code> property for dashboard sharing tokens.</li> <li>A <code>reportId</code> property for report sharing tokens</li> </ul>  |
|creationDate|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Token creation date in ISO 8601 format (<code>yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSS'Z'</code>) |
|enabled|[boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Boolean)|The token is enabled (<code>true</code>) or disabled (<code>false</code>). |
|expirationDate|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>Token expiration date in ISO 8601 format (<code>yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSS'Z'</code>).</p> <p>If not set, the token never expires.</p>  |
|id|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The ID of the token, consisting of prefix and public part of the token. |
|lastUsedDate|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Token last used date in ISO 8601 format (<code>yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSS'Z'</code>) |
|lastUsedIpAddress|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Token last used IP address. |
|modifiedDate|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|Token last modified date in ISO 8601 format (<code>yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSS'Z'</code>). Updating scopes or name counts as modification, enabling or disabling a token does not. |
|name|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The name of the token. |
|owner|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The owner of the token. |
|personalAccessToken|[boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Boolean)|The token is a <a href="https://dt-url.net/wm03sop" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" >personal access token</a> (<code>true</code>) or an API token (<code>false</code>). |

### ApiTokenCreate

Parameters of a new API token.

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|expirationDate|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>The expiration date of the token.</p> <p>You can use one of the following formats:</p> <ul> <li>Timestamp in UTC milliseconds.</li> <li>Human-readable format of <code>2021-01-25T05:57:01.123+01:00</code>. If no time zone is specified, UTC is used. You can use a space character instead of the <code>T</code>. Seconds and fractions of a second are optional.</li> <li>Relative timeframe, back from now. The format is <code>now-NU/A</code>, where <code>N</code> is the amount of time, <code>U</code> is the unit of time, and <code>A</code> is an alignment. The alignment rounds all the smaller values to the nearest zero in the past. For example, <code>now-1y/w</code> is one year back, aligned by a week. You can also specify relative timeframe without an alignment: <code>now-NU</code>. Supported time units for the relative timeframe are: <ul> <li><code>m</code>: minutes</li> <li><code>h</code>: hours</li> <li><code>d</code>: days</li> <li><code>w</code>: weeks</li> <li><code>M</code>: months</li> <li><code>y</code>: years</li> </ul> </li> </ul> <p>If not set, the token never expires. Ensure that the expiration date is not set in the past.</p>  |
|name<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The name of the token. |
|personalAccessToken|[boolean](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Boolean)|<p>The token is a personal access token (<code>true</code>) or an API token (<code>false</code>).</p> <p>Personal access tokens are tied to the permissions of their owner.</p>  |
|scopes<sup>*required</sup>|Array&lt;"ActiveGateCertManagement" &#124; "AdvancedSyntheticIntegration" &#124; "CaptureRequestData" &#124; "DTAQLAccess" &#124; "DataExport" &#124; "DataImport" &#124; "DataPrivacy" &#124; "Davis" &#124; "DssFileManagement" &#124; "ExternalSyntheticIntegration" &#124; "InstallerDownload" &#124; "LogExport" &#124; "PluginUpload" &#124; "ReadConfig" &#124; ### ApiTokenCreated

The newly created token.

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|expirationDate|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The token expiration date in ISO 8601 format (<code>yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSS'Z'</code>). |
|id|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The ID of the token, consisting of prefix and public part of the token. |
|token|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The secret of the token. |

### ApiTokenList

A list of API tokens.

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|apiTokens|Array&lt;<a href="#apitoken">ApiToken</a>&gt;|A list of API tokens. |
|nextPageKey|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|<p>The cursor for the next page of results. Has the value of <code>null</code> on the last page.</p> <p>Use it in the <strong>nextPageKey</strong> query parameter to obtain subsequent pages of the result.</p>  |
|pageSize|[number](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Number)|The number of entries per page. |
|totalCount<sup>*required</sup>|[number](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/Number)|The total number of entries in the result. |

### ApiTokenSecret

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|token<sup>*required</sup>|[string](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Glossary/String)|The API token. |
